私、辻村恒星が七月家に婿入りしたのは、三十を幾つか過ぎた頃だった。: It was when I was a few years past thirty that I, Seiji Tsujimura, married into the Nanatsuki family as a groom.
それまで私は曲を書いて生計を立てており、偶に欧州へ渡っては友人と交流を深めたりも: Until then, I had made a living composing music, occasionally traveling to Europe to deepen my friendships with colleagues there.
それは仙台で行われた演奏会の終了後だった。見目麗しい女性が私に話し掛けてきたのだ: It happened after a concert held in Sendai. A beautiful woman approached me.
彼女は演奏会の出資者だった。私の曲に大層感銘を受けたそうだ。私は社交辞令と受け取: She was a sponsor of the concert. She said she was deeply moved by my compositions. I took it as a social pleasantry, but apparently, it was not so for her.
後日、私は七月家に招待され、そこで求婚されたのだ。: Later, I was invited to the Nanatsuki estate, and there, she proposed to me.
七月家は女系の一族で、代々余所から婿を取っていた。美しい彼女に惹かれていた私に断: The Nanatsuki family was a matriarchal clan that had for generations taken husbands from the outside. I, attracted to her beauty, had no reason to refuse.
そうして私は、彼女、七月摩夜と結ばれて七月恒星となった。: And so I was united with her, Maya Nanatsuki, and became Kousei Nanatsuki.
結婚生活は幸せだった。日々の暮らしに悩まされる事なく、気儘に曲を書いていられた。: Our married life was happy. I was able to write music at my leisure, untroubled by the concerns of daily life.
私は摩夜を深く愛していた。しかし子供は長らく授かれなかった。それだけが唯一の心残: I loved Maya deeply. However, for a long time, we were not blessed with children. That was my only regret.
私にはディートリッヒ・リーシェと云う、ドイツ人の友人が居た。彼も作曲家で、婦人も: I had a German friend named Dietrich Lische. He too was a composer, and his wife was a renowned singer.
そのふたりが神戸で事故に遭ったと云う報せが届いたのは、摩夜と結婚してから数年後の: It was several years after my marriage to Maya that news reached me that the two of them had met with an accident in Kobe.
神戸へ駆けつけた私が見たのは、ふたりの遺体と残されたひとり娘だった。: Rushing to Kobe, I saw their two bodies and the daughter they had left behind.
身寄りのない外国人の子供を残しておくわけにもいかず、私は彼女を七憑館へ連れ帰った: I could not leave a kinless foreign child alone, so I brought her back with me to the Nanatsuki mansion.
子供の出来なかった摩夜は大層喜んで呉れたが、それが本心からだったのか、この時は疑: Maya, who had no children of her own, was overjoyed, but at the time, I could not even suspect whether her feelings were sincere.
少女の名はマリノと云った。亡き母親に似て、とても声の綺麗な少女だった。: The girl's name was Marino. Like her late mother, she was a girl with a very beautiful voice.
私たちは養子として迎えるつもりだったのだが、彼女は頑として聞き入れず、召使いとし: We intended to welcome her as an adopted child, but she adamantly refused, offering instead to work as a servant.
外国人として引け目があるのだろうと感じていた。当時は戦争の気運も高まっていた為か: I felt she might have had a sense of inferiority as a foreigner. Perhaps because the fervor for war was rising at the time, there was a movement toward excluding foreigners.
しかし彼女はドイツ人、同盟国の人間だ。何も気にすることはない筈だ。しかし彼女が首: Yet she was German, a person of an allied nation. There should have been no need for concern. However, she never nodded her assent.
マリノが七月家に来たのと同じ頃、私の弟の星次が使用人として雇われていた。: Around the same time Marino came to the Nanatsuki house, my younger brother, Seiji, was hired as a servant.
彼は画家を志し活動をしていたが、娘が病に倒れ、莫大な治療費が必要となった為に私が: He had been active as an aspiring painter, but after his daughter fell ill and massive medical expenses became necessary, I hired him.
マリノはよく働いた。元々いた召使いよりもずっとよく仕事をこなしていた。: Marino worked hard. She handled her duties far better than the servants who had been there before.
しかしそれを面白く思わない者がいた。誰あろう、摩夜だった。彼女はマリノが私に色目: However, there was someone who did not look upon this with pleasure. None other than Maya. She repeatedly slandered Marino, claiming she was casting flirtatious glances at me.
それは彼女の被害妄想だった。子が成せぬ事を自らの責と考え、衰えていく自らの身体に: It was merely her paranoia. She viewed the inability to conceive as her own fault and was losing confidence in her own fading body.
彼女は奇矯な振る舞いをすることが多くなった。夜中に徘徊し、暴れる事もあった。: She began to exhibit eccentric behavior more frequently. She would wander at night and sometimes become violent.
それは七月家に代々受け継がれている病だった。七月家は代々近親婚を繰り返しており、: It was a disease passed down through generations of the Nanatsuki family. The Nanatsuki family had repeatedly practiced consanguineous marriage for generations, and various abnormalities began to manifest because of it.
女系であることも異常のひとつであった。私達が子を成せなかったのもその影響があるか: The matriarchal lineage was also one of those abnormalities. The fact that we could not conceive may also have been an influence of that.
その頃から、村で若い娘の失踪が相次いで発生するようになった。大地主であった七月家: Around that time, disappearances of young women began to occur one after another in the village. As the great landowners, the Nanatsuki family received requests for searches time and again.
私はこの先の事を書き残すべきか迷っている。愛した妻を告発するような真似が果たして: I hesitate whether I should leave a record of what follows. Would such an act as accusing the wife I loved ever be forgiven?
しかし、当時官憲に通報しなかった私にも多大なる罪がある。私はここに書き残すことで: However, I, who did not report her to the authorities at the time, am also burdened with heavy sin. I wish to make my confession by writing it here.
彼女は、七月摩夜は吸血鬼であったのだ。: She, Maya Nanatsuki, was a vampire.
無論西洋にある吸血鬼伝説とは違う。彼女は歴とした人間だ。ただ、七月家の血が彼女を: Of course, it is different from the vampire legends of the West. She is a full-fledged human. It is merely that the Nanatsuki blood is driving her mad.
彼女は若い娘を館に連れてきた。そして地下の機械室の奥にある扉から中に入っていくの: She would bring young women to the mansion. Then she would enter through the door at the back of the machine room in the basement.
娘は出て来なかった。摩夜ひとりが充実した笑みを浮かべて出てくるのだ。: The girls never came back out. Maya alone would emerge, wearing a satisfied smile.
数箇月に一度、彼女は若い娘の血を啜り、自らの穢れた血を浄化していたのだ。: Once every few months, she would sip the blood of a young woman to purify her own tainted blood.
私は、彼女の行為に目を瞑っていた。ひとたび耐えれば、また彼女は美しくて穏和な摩夜: I turned a blind eye to her actions. If I endured it once, she would return to being the beautiful and gentle Maya once more.
だが、彼女も慚愧の念に堪えなかったのだろう。一年以上若い娘を連れてこない日が続い: Yet she too must have been unable to bear the weight of her remorse. Days when she did not bring a young woman continued for over a year.
私は安堵した。呪われた血が遂に浄化されたのだと思った。: I felt relieved. I thought the cursed blood had finally been purified.
だがそれは、ただの希望でしかなかったのだ。: But that was nothing more than a wish.
村ではどの家でも警戒を怠らぬようになり、若い娘を家から出さぬように、あるいは逆に: In the village, every household had become vigilant, refusing to let young women out of the house, or conversely, sending them to work in the city.
彼女が娘を連れてこなかったのは、ただ若い娘が見付からなかっただけなのだ。: The reason she did not bring a girl was simply because no young woman could be found.
彼女は血の乾きを隠さぬようになった。ついには私の見ている前で、美しく成長したマリ: She no longer hid her thirst for blood. Finally, before my very eyes, she attacked Marino, who had grown into a beautiful woman.
私はすぐに摩夜を隔離した。裏庭に塔を築かせ、その二階に彼女を幽閉した。: I immediately isolated Maya. I had a tower built in the backyard and imprisoned her on its second floor.
血を啜る事を止めさせんが為、御巫士郎と云う医者を招いて治療に当たらせた。: In order to make her stop sipping blood, I invited a doctor named Shiro Mikanagi to attend to her treatment.
彼女の精神が落ち着きを欲する時は必ず肉体を求めてきた。私はそれに出来る限り応える: Whenever her spirit craved stability, she would inevitably seek my flesh. I did my best to respond to that. For I still loved her.
ここでこの七月家の事を記しておこう。: Let me record here the matter of this Nanatsuki family.
私がこの部屋を発見したとき、ここには一冊の書物が遺されていた。代々の当主が綴った: When I discovered this room, a single book had been left here. it was the secret of the Nanatsuki family, penned by successive heads of the house.
私はそれを読み、私なりに分析をした。七月家の呪われし血が、この小さな世界を壊して: I read it and analyzed it in my own way. The cursed blood of the Nanatsuki family was breaking this small world.
七月家の初代当主は陰秘学に傾倒し、当時住んでいた屋敷を黒く塗り固めた。これが七憑: The first head of the Nanatsuki family was devoted to occultism and painted the mansion where he lived black. This was the beginning of the Nanatsuki mansion.
純血を遺すために実の妹と契り、娘を産ませた。さらにはその娘とも情を交わし、近親婚: To preserve pure blood, he lay with his own younger sister and had her bear a daughter. Furthermore, he shared intimacy with that daughter as well, attempting to create a clan through inbreeding.
結論から申せば、その試みは失敗に終わった。元々の因子故か、何代か経つと異常を持つ: To state the conclusion, that attempt ended in failure. Perhaps because of the original factors, after several generations, children with abnormalities began to appear.
女子しか産まれなくなったのもその所為と思われる。やがて七月家は、異常の現れた子を: The fact that only females were born is also thought to be because of that. Eventually, the Nanatsuki family began to exclude children who showed abnormalities.
穢れた血を浄化する為と、異常の現れた子を『贄』とした。棺に入れられ首を切られた。: To purify the tainted blood, children who showed abnormalities were made into 'Sacrifices.' They were placed in coffins and decapitated.
異常の現れぬ子を遺そうと、外部からも血を加えた。しかし一度遺された因子は中々消え: In an attempt to leave children who did not show abnormalities, blood was also added from the outside. However, the factors once left behind did not easily disappear.
しかし時代は移り変わり、『贄』も変化した。平静である事を婚姻の儀の際に証明する形: Yet the times changed, and the 'Sacrifice' changed as well. It took the form of proving one's tranquility during the marriage ceremony.
我が妻、摩夜も、婚姻の儀の際に棺へ入り、一晩をそこで過ごした。その夜には何事も起: My wife, Maya, also entered a coffin during the marriage ceremony and spent a night there. Nothing happened that night, and her innocence was proven.
それでも彼女の異常は、しっかりと残っていたのだ。: Yet her abnormality remained firmly within her.
話を戻そう。一向に容態が快復しない摩夜の事で、徐々に私の精神も狂気に蝕まれていた: Let us return to the story. Due to Maya's condition, which showed no sign of recovery, my own spirit was gradually eroded by madness.
一度だけ、私は過ちを犯した。: Once, and only once, I committed an error.
私は、マリノと関係を持った。: I had a relationship with Marino.
何も弁明は出来ない事だ。彼女が私に好意を寄せていたのはわかっていた。だがそれは父: There is no excuse I can offer. I knew she harbored affection for me. But I thought it was a feeling toward fatherhood.
否、思おうとしていた。: No, I was trying to think so.
或いはそれに摩夜は気付いていたのかもしれない。だからこその讒言、だからこその狂気: Or perhaps Maya had noticed it. Was that the reason for her slander, and the reason for her madness?
ならばその罪は全て私の責であろう。私はこの先ずっと摩夜を、七月家を守らねばならな: If so, all that sin is my responsibility. I had to protect Maya and the Nanatsuki family from then on.
そう、産まれてくる子の為にも。: Yes, for the sake of the child to be born as well.
私は摩夜が身籠もったのを主治医の御巫から聞かされた。: I was informed by my primary physician, Mikanagi, that Maya was pregnant.
長年子を成すことが出来なかった為半ば諦めていたのだが、実際に妊娠の事実を知ると私: Having been unable to conceive for many years, I had half given up, but when I learned the fact of her pregnancy, I was very happy.
愛する妻との間に出来た子を喜ばぬ筈がなかった。: There was no reason not to rejoice over a child conceived with my beloved wife.
それからの摩夜は人が変わったかのように大人しくなった。: From then on, Maya became as quiet as if she were a changed person.
否、昔の摩夜に戻ったのだ。: No, she returned to the Maya of old.
しかし、その喜びも長くは続かなかった。: However, that joy did not last long.
マリノが姿を消したのだ。残された書き置きは紛うことなくマリノの文字だった。理由も: Marino vanished. The note left behind was unmistakably in Marino's handwriting. No reason or anything else was written.
方々手を尽くしてマリノの行方を探したが一向に見付からなかった。: I exhausted every means to search for Marino's whereabouts, but she was never found.
私は摩夜を疑った。しかし彼女は子を授かった喜びに満たされていた。マリノの事など眼: I suspected Maya. But she was filled with the joy of being blessed with a child. She seemed to have no concern for Marino.
私はまた罪を重ねてしまったのだ。愛する妻を疑ってしまったのだ。彼女はずっと塔の中: I had layered sin upon sin again. I had suspected my beloved wife. Since she had been in the tower the entire time, there was no way she could have done anything.
やがて子が生まれた。マリノのことは気懸かりだったが、それでもやっと我が子を授かれ: Before long, the child was born. Marino was still a concern, but even so, the joy of finally being blessed with my own child was exceptional.
娘は藍と名付けられ、すくすくと育った。やがて次女の紅緒も生まれた。: The daughter was named Ai and grew up healthy. Eventually, the second daughter, Benio, was also born.
今思えば、この時が私の幸せの最高潮だったのだろう。: Looking back now, that time must have been the peak of my happiness.
勿論、マリノの事がなければではあるが。: Of course, provided the matter of Marino were not considered.
紅緒が生まれ、摩夜は塔から出てきた。彼女の妄執は収まり、慈愛の聖母もかくやと云う: After Benio was born, Maya emerged from the tower. Her obsession had subsided, and she seemed as fulfilled as a holy mother of mercy.
彼女は館にマリノが居ない事を気にしていたようであった。しかしそれについて私に尋ね: She seemed concerned that Marino was not in the mansion. However, she never questioned me about it.
だから私は安心していたのだ。: That is why I felt at ease.
暫くして、館から少し離れた森の中にあった山小屋で火災が発生した。: A short while later, a fire broke out in a mountain cabin located in the forest a short distance from the mansion.
通りがかった青年が火中に飛びこみ、残されていたふたりの子供を助け出した。: A young man who happened to be passing by jumped into the flames and rescued the two children left inside.
しかしその母親らしき人は助けられず、死亡した。: However, the person who appeared to be their mother could not be saved and died.
青年は七月家の顧問弁護士を務めていた高嶺の息子、仁だった。: The young man was Jin, the son of Takamine, who served as the legal counsel for the Nanatsuki family.
彼は貌に火傷を負った。: He suffered burns to his face.
子供も、姉が背中に酷い火傷を負っていた。: Of the children, the older sister suffered terrible burns on her back.
そのふたりの娘を目にしたとき、私は全てを悟った。: When I laid eyes on those two daughters, I realized everything.
私とのたった一度の過ちでこの世に生をなした、哀れな双子だった。: They were piteous twins brought into this world by my single error.
亡くなった母親はマリノだったのだ。: The mother who died was Marino.
私はまた、自らの身勝手な行いによって罪を重ねていたのだ。: Once again, I had layered sin upon sin through my own selfish actions.
私は罪を償わなければならなかった。: I had to atone for my sins.
孤児となった娘達を引き取り、今度こそきちんと私達の子供とした。: I took in the daughters who had become orphans and, this time for certain, made them properly our children.
姉を歌音、妹を詩音と名付けた。: I named the older sister Kanon and the younger sister Shion.
重傷で意識の無かった歌音を街の病院へ入院させ、一切の記憶を喪っていた詩音は館で静: I had Kanon, who was seriously injured and unconscious, admitted to a hospital in the city, and let Shion, who had lost all her memories, convalesce at the mansion.
何も言わぬ摩夜だったが、詩音を構うこともなかった。詩音は星次が面倒を見る事となっ: Though Maya said nothing, she never bothered with Shion either. It was decided that Seiji would look after Shion.
詩音は以前摩夜が幽閉されていた塔に住まう事になった。多大な負い目がある私は、摩夜: Shion came to live in the tower where Maya had previously been imprisoned. I, burdened with heavy debt, could say nothing to Maya's decision.
私は、娘達に囲まれていたが、以前ほどの幸せを享受する事が出来なくなっていた。: I was surrounded by my daughters, but I was no longer able to enjoy the same happiness as before.
マリノの死の原因が、摩夜にあると知ってしまったからだ。: For I had learned that the cause of Marino's death lay with Maya.
私とマリノが関係した事を知った摩夜は、マリノの居所を突き止め、その山小屋に火を放: Upon learning of the relationship between Marino and me, Maya had tracked down Marino's whereabouts and set fire to that mountain cabin.
それからの私は、ただ無機質な日々を過ごすだけの抜け殻だった。曲も作らず、無駄に呼: From then on, I was merely a hollow shell spending sterile days. I wrote no music and merely breathed in vain.
塔に幽閉されていた詩音は、身体中の色素が抜け落ちていた。: Shion, who was imprisoned in the tower, had lost all pigment from her body.
白く透明な肌はどうしてもマリノを思い起こさせ、私を更に憂鬱にさせた。: Her white, transparent skin inevitably reminded me of Marino, making me even more melancholy.
それに加え、病院から歌音が居なくなったとの連絡があった。いったい何が起こったのか: In addition, news came from the hospital that Kanon had disappeared. What on earth had happened? I did not even know that much.
私は病んでいた。精神だけではなく、肉体すらも。徐々に衰弱していく身体に、私はこれ: I was ill. Not only in spirit, but in body as well. In my gradually weakening body, I realized that illness was not the only cause.
私の診察は御巫士郎が行っていた。彼は私に毒を盛っていたのだ。: My examinations were performed by Shiro Mikanagi. He was poisoning me.
極々微量の砒素が食事に混ぜられていた。恐らく摩夜の指示なのだろう。: Infinitesimal amounts of arsenic were mixed into my meals. Likely at Maya's instruction.
ただ、彼と摩夜の間にあった関係こそが、私を決定的に壊す要因となった。ふたりの娘、: However, the relationship between him and Maya was the factor that decisively broke me. The two daughters, Ai and Benio, were children conceived between Maya and Shiro Mikanagi.
勿論ふたりの子供に罪はない。私は努めて平静を装い、娘達に接した。: Of course, the two children are blameless. I endeavored to maintain a facade of tranquility and interacted with my daughters.
しかし、何処かで私の子ではないと云う感情があったのだろう。娘達には本当に済まない: However, there must have been a feeling somewhere that they were not my children. I feel truly sorry toward my daughters.
だが、どうしても詩音だけは守りたい。マリノを守れなかった私に出来る最後の事だ。: Yet, I wish to protect Shion at all costs. It is the last thing I can do, having failed to protect Marino.
星次に詩音の事を託した。弟ならば詩音を守ってくれるだろう。: I entrusted Shion to Seiji. My brother will surely protect Shion.
私は、衰弱して静かな死を迎えるよりも、誇り高き死を選ぶ。: Rather than meeting a quiet death through debilitation, I choose a death with pride.
これを読む者よ。七月家が抱える全ての妄執を断ち切り、真実を明るみにして欲しい。: Let the one who reads this sever all the obsessions harbored by the Nanatsuki family and bring the truth to light.
意気地のない私を嗤っても構わない。: You may laugh at my spinelessness.
心残りは、歌音の行方が知れぬ事だ。どうぞ無事生きていて欲しい。そしてどうぞ、摩夜: My regret is that Kanon's whereabouts are unknown. I pray she is alive and well. And I pray that she does not hate Maya.
私はこの期に及んでも、彼女の事を愛しているのだ。: $ Even at this late stage, I still love her.
地下へ降りてきた。: We went down to the basement.
智士「ここの奥でしたよね……」: Satoshi: "It was at the back of this place, wasn't it...?"
なるみ「扉があったなんて知らなかったわね……見たこともないし、恐らく埋められてい: Narumi: "I never knew there was a door... I've never seen one, so it must be buried... right there."
そこだけ別の漆喰で塗り固められたようになっていた。: That spot alone looked as if it had been sealed up with a different kind of plaster.
ぼくは手近な所に置かれていた金梃を拾い上げた。: I picked up a crowbar that had been left nearby.
悩んでいる時間が勿体ない。: Wasting time worrying about it was out of the question.
奥から無骨な鋼鉄製の扉が出てきた。: From behind it, a rugged steel door emerged.
なるみ「……書かれていた通りね。この奥に……奥様が吸血鬼だって証拠があるの？」: Narumi: "...It's just as written. Is the evidence that the Madam is a vampire behind here?"
智士「……入ればわかる事です」: Satoshi: "...We'll find out once we go in."
ぼくは扉に手を掛けた。: I placed my hand on the door.
真っ暗な空間だった。: It was a space of pure darkness.
懐中電灯を向けると、扉の右側に細い廊下が続いていた。: When I pointed the flashlight, a narrow corridor continued to the right of the door.
何ともいえない臭いが漂っている。: An indescribable odor drifted through the air.
長時間空気が動いていない、澱んだ臭いだ。: It was a stagnant smell, as if the air hadn't moved for a long time.
智士「……摩夜さんはこの奥に若い娘を連れ込んでいたそうです」: Satoshi: "...It seems Maya-san would bring young women back into this place."
慎重に歩を進めながらぼくは話し出す。: I began to speak as I moved forward cautiously.
智士「洋の東西を問わず、処女には特別な力が宿るとされてきました。それを摩夜さんが: Satoshi: "Regardless of East or West, it has been said that special power resides within virgins. It wouldn't be strange if Maya-san craved that."
なるみ「……女の子はここから出て来なかったのよね」: Narumi: "...And the girls never came back out, did they?"
智士「そのようですね……つまりこの奥には……」: Satoshi: "So it seems... which means at the end of this..."
暗闇の中、なるみさんが唾を飲み込んだ音が聞こえた。: In the darkness, I heard the sound of Narumi-san swallowing hard.
何度か角を折れて進んでいくと、また扉が現れた。: After turning several corners and moving forward, another door appeared.
……そこは、他のどの部屋とも違っていた。: ...That place was different from any other room.
この『七憑館』の全ての負の部分を凝縮したような、空気の濁った部屋だった。: It was a room with stagnant air, as if every negative aspect of this 'Nanatsuki Mansion' had been concentrated there.
床には骨が幾つも散乱していた。: Several bones were scattered on the floor.
明らかに人のものだとわかる頭蓋骨も転がっていた。: Skulls that were clearly human were also rolling about.
部屋の隅には、衣服を纏った儘の、比較的原型が残っている白骨死体も残されていた。: In the corner of the room, a skeletal corpse with relatively intact form, still draped in clothing, remained.
なるみ「……あたしやあかねの先輩みたいね」: Narumi: "...She looks like a senior colleague to me and Akane."
なるみさんが着ているものと同じ服だった。: It was the same clothing Narumi-san was wearing.
ぼくは吐き出しそうになるのを何とかこらえていた。: I did my best to suppress the urge to vomit.
無論そう感じたのは原形を留めていたからに過ぎないのだけど――: Of course, I only felt that way because it still retained its shape—
なるみ「旦那様が書いた通りだったらね。奥様がお認めになるかしら……」: Narumi: "If it's as the Master wrote. I wonder if the Madam will admit to it..."
遺体の手が置かれた所に、白い布切れがある。: Where the corpse's hand lay, there was a scrap of white cloth.
黙祷を捧げた後、その布を手に取った。: After offering a silent prayer, I picked up the cloth.
智士「……同じようなものをあかねさんが持っていました。何か関係があるのでしょうが: Satoshi: "...Akane-san had something similar. There must be some connection..."

無人の事務室を通り抜け、外に出る。: I passed through the empty office and stepped outside.
星明かりが見えるほどではないが、雲も随分薄くなっているようだ。: The starlight wasn't visible yet, but the clouds seemed to have thinned out quite a bit.

――恒星さんの手記に書かれていた事か。: —Was it what was written in Kousei-san's journal?

恒星さんの手記を読んだ時から気になっていた。: It's been on my mind since I read Kousei-san's journal.
成程――彼女なら、復讐する理由はある。: I see—if it's her, she has a reason for revenge.
だとすると、狙いは摩夜さんだろうか――: If so, is the target Maya-san—?

どのくらい時間が経っただろうか。: I wonder how much time has passed.
ぼくは慌ただしく階段を下りてくる音に反応して格子窓から外を見た。: Reacting to the sound of someone descending the stairs in a hurry, I looked out through the grated window.
辻村「こんな所にいらっしゃったんですか……」: Tsujimura: "So this is where you were..."

演奏室に紅緒さんが――: Benio-san is in the performance room—
どのくらい壁を削り続けていたのだろうか。: I wonder how long I'd been scraping away at the wall.
不意に、ぼこっ、と穴が広がった。: Suddenly, a hole gaped open with a dull thud.
向こうの部屋が見える。: I can see the room on the other side.
どうやら機械室のようだ。: It appears to be the machine room.

なるみ「はあっ……何とか出られたわね」: Narumi: "Whew... we managed to get out."

突然扉が開き、辻村さんが姿を現した。: Suddenly the door opened, and Tsujimura-san appeared.
辻村さんに掴み掛からんばかりの勢いで訊ねる。: I asked with enough force to almost grab Tsujimura-san by the collar.

紅緒さんがこんな姿に変わってしまうなんて。: For Benio-san to be changed into such a state.

黒いピアノにも、鮮血が飛び散ってしまっている。: Fresh blood had splattered onto the black piano as well.
常日頃から綺麗にしていたせいか、指紋らしきものも見当たらない。: Perhaps because she always kept it clean, no fingerprint-like smudges were visible.

ピアノの鍵盤の上に俯せている紅緒さんの遺体。: Benio-san's corpse lay face down on the piano keys.

頭部の失われた紅緒さんの身体がピアノに伏せっている。: Benio-san's headless body lay face down on the piano.
一刻も早く紅緒さんを休ませてあげたかった。: I wanted to let Benio-san rest as soon as possible.

御巫先生とふたりで担架を運ぶ。: Dr. Mikanagi and I carried the stretcher together.
紅緒さんの遺体をベッドの上に寝かせる。: We laid Benio-san's body onto the bed.
紅緒さんの黒猫がベッドに上がり、遺体に身を寄せるようにして丸くなる。: Benio-san's black cat climbed onto the bed and curled up as if nestling against the body.

廊下に出て部屋に鍵を掛ける。: Stepping into the corridor, we locked the room.

ただ……紅緒さんに藍さん、それに六曜さんがいない。: Only... Benio-san, Ai-san, and Rokuyou-san were missing.
ただ……紅緒さんと六曜さんがいない。: Only... Benio-san and Rokuyou-san were missing.
言われて室内を見回す。: At her words, I looked around the room.

こんな事にはならなかったのかもしれないのに……: Maybe it wouldn't have turned out like this...

声を掛けながら扉を開ける。: I called out while opening the door.
廊下にはなるみさんが立っていた。: Narumi-san was standing in the corridor.

とはいえ、放っておく訳にもいかない。: Even so, I couldn't just leave it be.
智士「もう少し、ぼくも探してみます」: Satoshi: "I'll try searching a little more myself."
一時間ほど館内を回ってみよう。: I'll try making a round of the mansion for about an hour.
想子さんの部屋の扉が開いていた。: The door to Souko-san's room was open.
中から反応はない。: There was no response from inside.
追いついてきたなるみさんがそう言った。: Narumi-san, who had caught up, said so.

想子「そうね……ただ、何か確実な証拠がやっぱり必要だわ」: Souko: "True... however, we still need some kind of definitive proof."
智士「……やはり、薬を持ってきたと云う状況が一番怪しいと思います。コップも残され: Satoshi: "...After all, the circumstance of her bringing the medicine is the most suspicious. The cup was left behind too, and if we start from there—"
想子「朝に詩音さんが毒殺されかけたのはやっぱり自殺未遂と云う考え？」: Souko: "So you think Shion-san's attempted poisoning this morning was indeed a suicide attempt?"
智士「ええ……青酸カリがいくら飲みづらいとはいえ、無理やり飲まされた可能性の方が: Satoshi: "Yeah... no matter how hard potassium cyanide is to drink, isn't it more likely she was forced to drink it?"
想子「そうね……強制されても、負い目のある詩音さんが断るのは難しそう……」: Souko: "Right... even if forced, it would be difficult for Shion-san, who feels a sense of debt, to refuse..."
智士「……行きましょう。摩夜さんが詩音さんを殺したのはほぼ間違いないと思います」: Satoshi: "...Let's go. I think it's almost certain that Maya-san killed Shion-san."
智士「……藍さんじゃないでしょうか」: Satoshi: "...Don't you think it was Ai-san?"
想子「藍さん？ でも彼女はもう死んじゃってるわ。偽装なんて出来そうにないし……」: Souko: "Ai-san? But she's already dead. I don't see how she could have faked it..."
智士「それは……特にないんですけど……」: Satoshi: "Well... I don't really have one..."
想子「じゃあ駄目よ。ちゃんとした証拠を提示できないと」: Souko: "Then it's no good. You have to be able to present proper evidence."
智士「紅緒さんの可能性は……」: Satoshi: "What about the possibility of Benio-san...?"
想子「ありえないわよ。もう死んじゃっているし、首が無くなっていても、あの状況じゃ: Souko: "That's impossible. She's already dead, and even with the head missing, in that situation, you can't even suspect it was a fake."
智士「……詩音さんの自殺の可能性だって残っています」: Satoshi: "...The possibility of Shion-san's suicide still remains."
想子「そうね……無いとは言い切れないわ。でもどうして？」: Souko: "Right... it can't be ruled out entirely. But why?"
智士「それは……御巫先生の件でぼくが責めすぎてしまったからかもしれません。状況か: Satoshi: "It might be because I pushed her too hard regarding Dr. Mikanagi's case. Given the circumstances, I didn't think it could be anyone but her..."
想子「それで、居たたまれなくなって自殺……あるいは、疑われた事に絶望したか……」: Souko: "And so, unable to bear it, she committed suicide... or perhaps she fell into despair after being suspected..."
智士「ぼくにも……詩音さんが本当に御巫先生を殺したのか、わからなくなっています」: Satoshi: "I... I'm no longer sure if Shion-san really killed Dr. Mikanagi."
智士「……自殺、と云う事でいいんでしょうか……？」: Satoshi: "...Should we just settle on suicide...?"
想子「……他に証拠が出てきていないのだったら、それでいいんだと思うけど」: Souko: "...If no other evidence turns up, I suppose that's how it'll go."
智士「辻村さんが殺したと云う可能性はないでしょうか」: Satoshi: "Is there a possibility that Tsujimura-san killed her?"
想子「……確かに辻村さんはいつもこの事務室にいるし、詩音さんの所へは誰にも気付か: Souko: "...Certainly, Tsujimura-san is always in this office, and he could have gone to Shion-san's place without anyone noticing... but why?"
智士「それは……理由まではわかりません」: Satoshi: "Well... I don't know the reason."
想子「理由が無いのに疑っちゃ駄目よ」: Souko: "You shouldn't suspect someone when there's no reason."
智士「高嶺さんの可能性はないでしょうか」: Satoshi: "Is there a possibility of Takamine-san?"
想子「高嶺さんか……毒殺と云うイメージからは遠いのよね……」: Souko: "Takamine-san, huh... he doesn't really fit the image of a poisoner..."
智士「それでも弁護士なんですから、学はあると思うんです」: Satoshi: "Still, he's a lawyer, so he must have some education."
想子「法律の知識と薬物の知識はまるで別物よ。それに彼には詩音さんを殺す理由がまる: Souko: "Knowledge of the law and knowledge of drugs are entirely different things. Besides, I see absolutely no reason for him to kill Shion-san."
智士「御巫先生が毒物を入れた可能性はどうでしょうか。あらかじめ仕掛けておけば、自: Satoshi: "What about the possibility that Dr. Mikanagi planted the poison? If he set it up in advance, he could kill the target without doing anything himself..."
想子「可能性だけならあってもおかしくはないわ。でも……御巫先生はもう殺されちゃっ: Souko: "If it's just the possibility, it wouldn't be strange. But... Dr. Mikanagi has already been killed, you know?"
智士「だから殺される前に仕掛けていたんですよ。それなら……死んだ後でも実行は可能: Satoshi: "That's why I'm saying he set it up before he was killed. That way... it's possible to carry it out even after death."
想子「じゃあ詩音さんを殺す理由は？ それこそ何もないわよ。まさか自分が殺される事: Souko: "Then what's the reason for killing Shion-san? There's absolutely nothing. Surely you aren't thinking that he predicted his own murder?"
想子「どうかしら。詩音さんを殺す理由が見当たらないけれど……」: Souko: "I wonder. I don't see any reason for her to kill Shion-san..."
智士「あかねさんは、御巫先生の事が好きだったようです。だから……復讐を果たしたと: Satoshi: "Akane-san seemed to be in love with Dr. Mikanagi. So... couldn't it be that she carried out her revenge?"
想子「この短時間で？ ちょっと無理があるんじゃないかしら」: Souko: "In this short amount of time? I think that's a bit of a stretch."
智士「なるみさんの可能性はないでしょうか」: Satoshi: "Is there a possibility it was Narumi-san?"
想子「……どうかしらね。理由がないように思えるわ。もしかしたら私達が知らない事が: Souko: "...I wonder. There doesn't seem to be a motive. Though there might be things we don't know."
想子「……ちょっと今の段階では絞れないわね」: Souko: "...We can't really narrow it down at this stage, can we?"
想子「そうね……もうすぐ夜も明けるし、しばらくは大人しくしていましょうか。麓の人: Souko: "Right... dawn is almost here, so let's just stay quiet for a while. Once the people from below arrive, we can have them call the police."
智士「そうですね……じゃあぼくは一旦部屋に戻ります」: Satoshi: "You're right... then I'll head back to my room for now."
想子「ええ。……鍵は掛けておくのよ」: Souko: "Yeah. ...Make sure you lock the door."
想子さんと別れ、階段を上って北側の廊下へ。: I parted ways with Souko-san, climbed the stairs, and headed for the northern corridor.
さすがにかなり眠くなっていた。: I was starting to get incredibly sleepy.
ぼうっとした頭で自分の部屋の前に立ち、鍵を取り出した時だった。: I was standing in front of my room with a dazed head, just as I pulled out my key.
何かと思って振り返ろうと身体を捻った途端、もの凄い激痛が襲ってきた。: Just as I twisted my body to look back and see what it was, a tremendous, sharp pain surged through me.
目の前に、長いスカートの裾が見えた。: Before my eyes, I saw the hem of a long skirt.
痛む背中に手をやると、指先に硬い物が触れた。: When I reached for my aching back, my fingertips touched something hard.
どろりと熱い液体が溢れ出している。: A thick, hot liquid was pouring out.
痛みに耐えながらぼくは彼女を見上げた。: Enduring the pain, I looked up at her.
――なんだかよく判らない、硬くて重い物がぼくの頭に打ち下ろされてかき消された。: —was wiped out by a hard, heavy object that was slammed down onto my head.
二階に上がり、摩夜さんの部屋の前へ。: I went upstairs and stood before Maya-san's room.
智士「摩夜さん、起きてらっしゃいますか？ ちょっとお話が――」: Satoshi: "Maya-san, are you awake? I have something to—"
声をかけ終わる前に扉が開いた。: Before I could finish speaking, the door opened.
いつもと変わらない摩夜さんが顔を出す。: Maya-san appeared, looking the same as always.
彼女は平静を装っている――それは、この異常な状況ではさらに奇妙に見えた。: She was maintaining a facade of tranquility—which looked even stranger in this abnormal situation.
摩夜「どうぞ中へ。廊下は寒いでしょう？」: Maya: "Please, come in. It must be cold in the hallway, no?"
ぼくの背中を押すようにして、想子さんに部屋の中へ押し込まれる。: Souko-san pushed me from behind, ushering me into the room.
摩夜「申し訳ありませんね、つい先程まで眠っておりましたもので」: Maya: "My apologies, I was asleep until just a moment ago."
笑みを浮かべながら摩夜さんはぼくたちを招き入れた。: Wearing a smile, Maya-san invited us in.
摩夜「何かお飲みになられますかしら？」: Maya: "Would you like something to drink?"
想子「詩音さんが何者かによって殺されました」: Souko: "Shion-san has been killed by someone."
ぼくが声を掛けようとしたのを制し、想子さんは単刀直入に切り出した。: Interrupting my attempt to speak, Souko-san got straight to the point.
智士「……ぼくは先程、詩音さんの部屋へ向かう摩夜さんを見ています。薬を持っていっ: Satoshi: "...I saw you heading toward Shion-san's room earlier. You said you were bringing her medicine...?"
摩夜「ええ、もちろんですわ。詩音が苦しんでいるようでしたから」: Maya: "Yes, of course. Shion seemed to be suffering, after all."
智士「……何の薬ですか？」: Satoshi: "...What kind of medicine was it?"
摩夜「楽になれるお薬ですわ。頭痛によく効くんですのよ」: Maya: "Medicine that can grant ease. It's very effective for headaches."
想子「生憎ですが、シアン化合物は頭痛の薬にはなりません」: Souko: "Unfortunately, cyanide is not a cure for headaches."
まるで慈愛の聖母のように、摩夜さんは微笑みを崩さない。: Like a holy mother of mercy, Maya-san's smile never wavered.
摩夜「ですが詩音は喜んで飲んでくれましたわ。お母さまありがとうございますと言いな: Maya: "But Shion drank it quite happily. She said, 'Thank you, Mother,' and drank it with such relish—"
摩夜「御巫先生が亡くなられてしまいましたので、私がお薬も用意しなくてはなりません: Maya: "Since Dr. Mikanagi has passed away, I had to prepare the medicine myself. Now that Ai and Benio are also dead..."
摩夜さんはまるで何かに酔っているかのように喋り続ける。: Maya-san continued to speak as if intoxicated by something.
摩夜「ただひとり詩音が残されてしまいましたので、私がきちんと世話をしなくてはなり: Maya: "Since Shion was the only one left, I had to look after her properly. It's only natural for a mother."
摩夜「それは、私の躾が至らなかったからですわ」: Maya: "That is because my discipline was insufficient."
摩夜「私は七月家の当主でございますから。家人の躾は私がしなくてはなりませんでした: Maya: "I am the head of the Nanatsuki family, after all. I had to discipline the members of my household..."
智士「今はそんな話をしている時じゃないでしょう！？」: Satoshi: "This is no time for that kind of talk!?"
摩夜「今はそう云う話をしているのでしょう？」: Maya: "But this *is* that kind of talk, isn't it?"
駄目だ……話がかみ合わない。: It was no use... we were talking past each other.
想子「――つまり、あなたは七月家の当主として、詩音さんを指導したと云う事かしら？: Souko: "—In other words, you 'guided' Shion-san as the head of the Nanatsuki family?"
それなのに、摩夜さんはまるで我が意を得たと言わんばかりの様子だ。: Yet, Maya-san looked as if she had finally been understood.
摩夜「そう――私は詩音を指導したんですの。あの子は大変な事をしてしまいましたから: Maya: "Yes—I guided Shion. That girl did something terrible, so it was only natural."
摩夜「あなたが仰いましたのでしょう？ 詩音が御巫先生を殺したと」: Maya: "Did you not say so yourself? That Shion killed Dr. Mikanagi."
……少なくとも、ぼくは御巫先生殺しの犯人が詩音さんだと、摩夜さんに言った覚えはな: ...At the very least, I didn't recall telling Maya-san that Shion-san was Dr. Mikanagi's killer.
でもそう考えて詩音さんに告げたのは確かだ。: But it was true that I had reached that conclusion and told Shion-san.
摩夜さんは恐らく、自分でその結論に達して――詩音さんを殺した。: Maya-san had likely reached that conclusion herself—and killed Shion-san.
摩夜「詩音は悪い子でしたわ。藍や紅緒まで……それに、結婚相手の勇さんも――おいた: Maya: "Shion was a bad girl. Even Ai and Benio... and even Isamu-san, her fiancé—a child who misbehaves needs to be punished, don't you agree...?"
想子「……全部、詩音さんがやったと仰るのですか……？」: Souko: "...Are you saying Shion-san did everything...?"
摩夜「他に考えられますか？ だからあの子は塔に入れていたのに……呪われた子なので: Maya: "Is there any other possibility? That's why I kept that girl in the tower... she was a cursed child, after all..."
智士「……そうかもしれません。でも……あなたが断罪していいと云うわけではありませ: Satoshi: "...That might be true. But... that doesn't mean you have the right to condemn her."
摩夜「どうして？ 私は七月家の当主、この土地を治める領主なのですよ？ならば私が判: Maya: "And why not? I am the head of the Nanatsuki family, the lord who rules this land. Is it not only natural that I am the one to pass judgment?"
智士「……そんな時代はとっくに終わっているんですよ。もう日本だって先進国の仲間入: Satoshi: "...That era ended a long time ago. Japan is a developed nation now."
摩夜「関係御座いませんわ。私こそがこの土地の司法であり、立法であり行政でもあるの: Maya: "That is irrelevant. I am the judiciary, the legislature, and the executive of this land."
摩夜「ふふっ……そうですわ、私こそが七月家の当主――この穢れた血を受け継ぐ者――: Maya: "Fufu... yes, I am the head of the Nanatsuki family—the one who inherits this tainted blood—"
摩夜「誰にも渡しはしない――藍にも紅緒にも詩音にも！ 全て、全て私のもの！！ 邪: Maya: "I will never give it to anyone—not to Ai, not to Benio, not to Shion! Everything, everything is mine!! Those who interfere—"
ぎらり、と、血走った目がぼくらを捉えた。: A glint caught her bloodshot eyes as they fixed on us.
摩夜「全て――あの男と同じように――」: Maya: "Will all—end up just like that man—"
底冷えのするような低い声で呟く。: She whispered in a low, chilling voice.
摩夜「その名を口にするなっ！！ 穢らわしい！！」: Maya: "Do not speak that name!! It is filthy!!"
想子「あなたから求婚したのではないの？ 彼の事が書かれた雑誌記事にはそう載ってい: Souko: "Weren't you the one who proposed to him? That's what it said in the magazine article about him."
摩夜「あの男は私を裏切った！ だから私は――」: Maya: "That man betrayed me! That's why I—"
摩夜「……ふふ、そうよ……だから――あの男も、あの女も――」: Maya: "...Fufu, yes... that's right... so—that man, and even that woman—"
想子「何を言っているのかよくわからないけど――」: Souko: "I don't really know what you're talking about, but—"
想子「……どうやら、私を誰かと勘違いしているみたいだけど――」: Souko: "...It seems she's mistaken me for someone else—"
苦笑いを浮かべ、想子さんは立ち止まる。: Wearing a wry smile, Souko-san stopped.
想子「もうすぐ夜が明けて、ここへ警察がやってくるわ。これだけの事件だもの、駐在だ: Souko: "Dawn is almost here, and the police will be coming. With an incident this large, it won't just be the local precinct; the prefectural police will be here as well—"
想子「そうすれば、いくらあなたでも言い逃れは出来ないでしょう？ まあ、あなたが殺: Souko: "Once they arrive, no matter who you are, you won't be able to talk your way out of it, will you? Well, the only one we can prove you killed is Shion-san, so—"
智士「……それでも、重罪です」: Satoshi: "...Even so, it's a serious crime."
想子「それでも、あなたは弁護するのかしら？」: Souko: "And will you still defend her?"
と、想子さんはぼくの背後――部屋の入口へ目をやった。: With that, Souko-san looked behind me—toward the room's entrance.
高嶺「……俺は七月家の弁護士だからな、出来るだけの事はする」: Takamine: "...I am the Nanatsuki family's lawyer, after all. I'll do what I can."
想子「……そう。それだったらあまり長くは入っていないわね」: Souko: "...I see. In that case, she probably won't be in for very long."
詰まらなさそうに想子さんは溜息混じりで洩らした。: Souko-san muttered with a bored sigh.
摩夜さんは身体を震わせて、まだ想子さんを凝視している。: Maya-san was still staring at Souko-san, her body trembling.
想子「……ひとまず彼女を拘束して。危険は少ないでしょうけど――逃走や自殺をされて: Souko: "...For now, restrain her. She shouldn't be too dangerous—but if she tries to escape or commit suicide, it'll just be more trouble."
それからぼくは、摩夜さんの腕を後ろ手に縛り、辻村さんに見張りを頼んで部屋に幽閉し: After that, I tied Maya-san's arms behind her back and asked Tsujimura-san to keep watch while we confined her to her room.
夜が明けてからは高嶺さんが落とされた吊り橋に向かい、麓からやって来た御者と話を付: Once dawn broke, Takamine-san headed for the destroyed suspension bridge, spoke with the driver who had come from the foot of the mountain, and brought back the police and workers to fix the bridge.
今は人が渡れる仮の橋を架けている最中だ。: Right now, they're in the middle of constructing a temporary bridge for people to cross.
智士「あ、どうも。いただきます」: Satoshi: "Ah, thank you. I'll have some."
あかね「今なるみさんが簡単なお料理を作っていますから」: Akane: "Narumi-san is making some simple dishes now."
智士「ええ……でも、あかねさんたちはこれからどうするんですか？」: Satoshi: "Yeah... but what are you all going to do now, Akane-san?"
智士「摩夜さんは恐らく逮捕されるでしょう。それに、他の人ももう居なくなってしまい: Satoshi: "Maya-san will likely be arrested. And besides, everyone else is gone..."
あかね「そうですね……わたしたちも出て行かなければならないかもしれませんね……」: Akane: "True... we might have to leave as well..."
何か心残りがあるのか、あかねさんは少しだけ寂しそうに呟いた。: Akane-san whispered with a hint of sadness, as if she had some lingering regret.
あかね「……ここから山を幾つか越えた所の小さな港町です。ですが……もう誰もいませ: Akane: "...A small port town across several mountains from here. But... there's no one left there. I have nowhere to go."
想子「ほらそこ、ナンパしないの」: Souko: "Hey there, no picking up girls."
智士「そ、そんなつもりじゃ――」: Satoshi: "I-I wasn't trying to do anything like that—"
なるみ「あかねだったらあたしが引き取ってあげるわよ」: Narumi: "I'll take Akane in if she needs it."
続けて広間に入ってきたなるみさんがそう言った。: Narumi-san, who had just entered the hall, spoke up.
なるみ「そりゃもちろん。……まああたしも、前に住んでたところ引き払っちゃってるけ: Narumi: "Of course. ...Well, I've already moved out of my previous place, though. Shall we look for a new one together?"
あかねさんは目に涙を溜めて頷いた。: Akane-san nodded, her eyes filling with tears.
智士「……ところで想子さん、これからどうします？」: Satoshi: "...By the way, Souko-san, what are you going to do now?"
想子「そうね……もう調査どころじゃなくなったし、帰ってもいいんだけど……たぶん警: Souko: "Well... since there's no point in investigating anymore, I could just go home... but I'll probably be called in by the police..."
なるみ「麓か街で宿を取った方がいいんじゃない？ 取り調べとかはここでやるわけじゃ: Narumi: "Shouldn't you find an inn at the foot of the mountain or in town? They won't be doing the interrogations here, after all."
想子「そうもいかないわよ。……コーラが無くなっちゃったし」: Souko: "I can't do that. ...I'm all out of cola."
まるでそれが一番深刻な問題かのように想子さんは呻いた。: Souko-san groaned, as if that were the most serious problem of all.
扉が開き、高嶺さんが入ってくる。: The door opened and Takamine-san entered.
高嶺「橋が出来たぞ。ひとりずつしか渡れないが」: Takamine: "The bridge is ready. Only one person can cross at a time, though."
なるみ「それじゃ、お別れかな」: Narumi: "I guess this is goodbye, then."
ぼくは想子さんに名前を呼ばれ、立ち上がる。: Souko-san called my name, and I stood up.
智士「それでは……お邪魔しました」: Satoshi: "Well then... thank you for everything."
――私はやるべき事を全てなし終えた。: —I have finished everything I needed to do.
最初からの予定通り、あの女から愛する者を全て奪い取った。: Just as planned from the beginning, I have stripped that woman of everything she loved.
……だのに、気分は晴れなかった。: ...And yet, my mood did not brighten.
復讐など無意味なことだから。: Revenge is a meaningless thing, after all.
だけど……気分は晴れないくせに、達成感には包まれていた。: But... even though my heart felt no lighter, I was enveloped in a sense of accomplishment.
これを成し遂げる為だけに今まで生きてきたから。: $ Because I have lived until now solely to achieve this.
それでも死者は戻らない。: Even so, the dead do not return.
でも……どれだけ無意味であろうと、今までの人生に取って、大切な指標だったのは確か: But... no matter how meaningless it may be, it was certainly a vital compass for my life until now.
今まで泥水を啜り生き存えてきたのは、この一瞬のためにあったのだから。: I have survived by swallowing mud all this time for the sake of this one moment.
だけど……やはり虚しかった。: But... as expected, it felt empty.
東京に戻った私は、今まで通りの生活を続けていた。: Returning to Tokyo, I continued my life as before.

ふたりの少女も集まってくる。: The two girls also gathered around.

「そうよ。昔々ね、雪には色が無かったの。せっかく降っても、誰にも見えなかったのよ: "That's right. A long, long time ago, snow had no color. Even if it fell, no one could see it."
「それで、雪は寂しくなって、神様にお願いしたの。自分にも色が欲しいって」: "So the snow grew lonely and asked God. It said it wanted a color for itself too."
「ええ、そう。神様は他のお花から色を貰いなさいって言ったのよ」: "Yes, that's right. God told it to get a color from the other flowers."
「ええ。スノードロップ――このお花がね、それなら私の色をさし上げますと言って、白: "Yes. The snowdrop—this flower said, 'In that case, I will give you my color,' and shared its white color with the snow."
「そうね……あなたたちも、このお花のように、困っている人がいたら助けてあげてね…: "Yes... I want you two to be like this flower and help people when they're in trouble, okay...?"
「うんっ！ ねえお母さま、お歌うたって！」: "Yeah! Hey Mommy, sing us a song!"
母親はゆっくりと、優しい歌声を響かせる。: The mother slowly began to sing with a gentle voice.
じんわりと心に染み渡る歌。: A song that seeped into the heart.
どうして、こんな事を今更思い出してしまったのだろう。: Why did I only remember this now?
白い雪の世界は消え、赤い世界が戻ってきた。: The world of white snow vanished, and the world of red returned.
もう声は出ていない。: Her voice was no longer coming out.
手足の感覚もない。: She had no sensation in her limbs.
こんなに哀しいのに、涙は出なかった。: Even though she was so sad, no tears came.
身体の水分は全て蒸発してしまった。: All the moisture in her body had evaporated.
復讐を完遂出来なかったのは残念だけど――: It was a shame she couldn't complete her revenge—
ただひとつ、可愛い後輩を欺いていた事を除いては。: Except for the one thing: deceiving her cute junior.
消火は間に合わなかった。: The fire couldn't be extinguished in time.
ぼくたちは、焼け落ちる七憑館を呆然と眺めている事しか出来なかった。: We could only stare blankly as the Nanatsuki mansion burned to the ground.
黒煙が空に伸びている。: Black smoke rose into the sky.
これが見えれば、麓から人がやってくるだろう。: If they saw this, people from the foot of the mountain would come.
ぼくは、想子さんと帰りたかったのに――: I wanted to go home with Souko-san—
耳を澄ませば、まだ想子さんの歌が聞こえてくるようだった。: If I listened closely, it felt as if I could still hear Souko-san's song.
でも聞こえてくるのは燃えさかる炎が爆ぜる音ばかりだった。: But all I could hear was the sound of the raging flames crackling.
智士「……紅緒さんの所為じゃありませんよ。それに……迷惑を掛けたのはぼくたちです: Satoshi: "...It's not your fault, Benio-san. Besides... we're the ones who caused trouble."
紅緒「しかし……そもそもの発端は七月家にあった。彼女が行動を起こしたのも、母上が: Benio: "But... the whole thing started with the Nanatsuki family. The reason she took action must have been because of my mother..."
智士「でも……他に方法はなかったんでしょうか……」: Satoshi: "But... was there no other way...?"
紅緒「……どうだろうな。母親の復讐を第一義としていたのならば、他の手段は選ばなか: Benio: "...I wonder. If her primary goal was revenge for her mother, she likely wouldn't have chosen any other means. That was her choice."
智士「だからって……藍さんや詩音さんまで……悲しすぎますよ、こんなの……」: Satoshi: "But still... even Ai-san and Shion-san... this is too sad..."
無言になり、ぼくらは燃え続ける館を眺めていた。: We fell silent, watching the mansion continue to burn.
想子さんも、摩夜さんも、詩音さんも、藍さんも、あかねさんも、六曜さんも――: The remains of Souko-san, Maya-san, Shion-san, Ai-san, Akane-san, and Rokuyou-san—
皆の遺体が荼毘に付されていた。: All their bodies were being cremated.
辻村「麓から人が来たようです。今高嶺様が橋の傍で応対をしております」: Tsujimura: "It seems people from below have arrived. Lord Takamine is currently speaking with them by the bridge."
紅緒「そうか……いつ頃下りられそうだ？」: Benio: "I see... when do you think we can go down?"
辻村「何とも言えません……何せ、犠牲者が多すぎますから……」: Tsujimura: "I cannot say... after all, there are far too many victims..."
紅緒「山を下りても、警察の厄介になりそうだな……仕方ないか」: Benio: "Even once we're off the mountain, it looks like we'll be in the hands of the police... I suppose it can't be helped."
智士「そうですね……何が起こったか、説明もしなくてはなりませんし」: Satoshi: "Right... we'll have to explain what happened, too."
辻村「そのあたりの事でしたら、高嶺様が何とかしてくださるとは思いますが……」: Tsujimura: "I'm sure Lord Takamine will handle that sort of thing for us..."
火の粉がこちらまで飛んできた。: Sparks flew all the way over to us.
紅緒「……もう少し離れた方が良さそうだな」: Benio: "...It might be better to move further away."
七憑館の前から離れる。: We moved away from the front of the Nanatsuki mansion.
智士「……紅緒さんは、この後どうするんですか？」: Satoshi: "...Benio-san, what are you going to do after this?"
紅緒「そうだな……家族も家も喪ってしまったからな。……そういや、はかせが兄だと判: Benio: "Well... I've lost both my family and my home. ...Oh, right, it was revealed that the doctor is my brother."
智士「じゃあ、御巫先生の所に厄介になるんですか？」: Satoshi: "Then, will you stay with Dr. Mikanagi?"
ぼくは橋の方にいる御巫先生をちらりと見た。: I stole a glance at Dr. Mikanagi, who was by the bridge.
なるみさんも向こうにいるようだ。: Narumi-san seemed to be over there as well.
紅緒「……今更それは出来まい。あくまで私は七月家の人間なのだからな」: Benio: "...I can't do that now. I'm a Nanatsuki, through and through."
辻村「あの……紅緒お嬢様さえよろしければ、東京に私の家がございますが……」: Tsujimura: "Um... if it would please Benio-ojousama, I have a house in Tokyo..."
紅緒「東京か……それも悪くないが……」: Benio: "Tokyo, huh... that's not bad either, but..."
紅緒「……うん、きみに付き合うのも悪くないな」: Benio: "...Yeah, hanging out with you might not be bad either."
智士「な……なんですかそれ……！？」: Satoshi: "Wha... what's that supposed to mean...!?"
紅緒「よし、決めた。これからよろしく頼むぞ」: Benio: "Alright, it's decided. I'll be counting on you from now on."
そう言って紅緒さんはぼくの背中を叩いてきた。: With that, Benio-san patted my back.
ちょうどよくなるみさんが戻ってきた。: Narumi-san returned at just the right time.
智士「じゃ、じゃあぼくは先に行ってます」: Satoshi: "I-I'll go ahead to the bridge then."
ぼくはすぐさま橋の方に歩き出した。: I immediately started walking toward the bridge.
紅緒さんと一緒に雪の道を走り。: Running along the snowy path with Benio-san.
不意にぼくは立ち止り、背後を見た。: Suddenly, I stopped and looked behind me.
ぼくは心の中で黙祷を捧げた。: I offered a silent prayer in my heart.
智士「……いえ、何でもありませんよ。それじゃ――行きましょう」: Satoshi: "...No, it's nothing. Well then—let's go."
警官「――では、六曜勇は<病<ヽ><死<ヽ><な<ヽ><さ<ヽ><れ<ヽ><た<: Police Officer: "——So, we are to conclude that Isamu Rokuyou passed away from a <sudden><illness>, correct?"
摩夜「ええ。感染症の懼れが御座いますので遺体はこちらで荼毘に付しましたわ」: Maya: "Yes. Due to the fear of infection, we have already cremated the remains here."
警官「かしこまりました。署長にもそう伝えておきます」: Police Officer: "Understood. I will convey that to the Chief as well."
御巫「いい訳がないさ。でもね、こういう事も時には必要なんだよ」: Mikanagi: "Of course it isn't. But you see, sometimes things like this are necessary."
納得は出来ないが、納得するしかないのだろう。: I couldn't accept it, but I suppose I had to.
少し離れたところにいる想子さんを見る。: I looked over at Souko-san, who was standing a short distance away.
彼女を告発出来なかったから。: Because I couldn't bring myself to accuse her.
だから、全てが有耶無耶にされてしまった。: Because of that, everything was being swept under the rug.
六曜さんは病死、藍さんは病気療養。: Rokuyou-san's death was an illness; Ai-san was away for medical recuperation.
詩音さんに至っては、塔の中に完全に幽閉されてしまった。: As for Shion-san, she was now completely imprisoned within the tower.
それは疑念ではなく、確信だった。: It wasn't suspicion anymore; it was a cold certainty.
今ここであの警官の前に飛び出して訴える事は出来るだろうか。: Could I jump out in front of that officer right now and scream the truth?
ぼくの言うことなど誰も聞いてはくれないだろう。: No one would listen to what I had to say.
ぼくはたった一度のチャンスを失ってしまったのだ。: I had lost my one and only chance.
想子「それじゃ、帰りましょうか」: Souko: "Well then, shall we head home?"
いけしゃあしゃあと想子さんは言う。: Souko-san said it with such brazen nonchalance.
まるで――本当に何も起こらなかったかのようだ。: As if——truly nothing had ever happened.
ぼくは自分の荷物だけ持った。: I picked up only my own luggage.
想子「あれ、私のは持ってくれないの？」: Souko: "Oh? You're not going to carry mine?"
智士「……どうせその指だって、骨なんて折れてないんでしょう？」: Satoshi: "......That finger of yours isn't even broken, is it?"
あっさりと想子さんは指に巻かれていた包帯を解いた。: Souko-san casually unraveled the bandage wrapped around her finger.
想子「だからって女の子に冷たくするのはよくないと思うわよ？」: Souko: "But still, I don't think it's very nice to be so cold to a girl, you know?"
先に歩き始めた想子さんを追ってぼくも歩き出す。: I started walking, following after Souko-san as she took the lead.
想子「筋はよかったと思うわよ。ただ詰めが甘いわ。焦らずにもう少し考えれば追い込め: Souko: "I think your reasoning was good. You just lacked the final touch. If you hadn't rushed and thought it through a bit more, you might have cornered me. But——"
想子さんがくるりと振り向いた。: Souko-san twirled around to face me.
想子「私に勝とうだなんて、十年早いのよ」: Souko: "You're ten years too early to think you could beat me."
やっぱり――想子さんには勝てそうにない。: I knew it——I stood no chance against Souko-san.
御巫「それは仕方ないよ。七月家に逆らえる警察官なんていないんだから」: Mikanagi: "It can't be helped. There isn't a policeman alive who can stand up to the Nanatsuki family."
紅緒「恐らく新聞にも載らずに詩音は釈放されるだろうよ。まだそれだけの力は残されて: Benio: "Shion will likely be released without even a mention in the newspapers. They still have that much influence left."
智士「それは……良いのか悪いのかわかりませんね……」: Satoshi: "I... I don't know if that's a good thing or a bad thing..."
御巫「詩音さんは黙秘を続けているからね……」: Mikanagi: "Shion-san has continued to maintain her silence, after all..."
想子「詩音さんもあれから何も喋っていないものね……」: Souko: "Shion-san hasn't said a word since it happened, has she...?"
紅緒「……とは云え、このまま何事も無かったように過ごす訳にもいくまい。……恐らく: Benio: "......That being said, we can't exactly act as if nothing happened. ......Shion will likely spend the rest of her life in confinement."
御巫「……この家は今までそうやってきたんだよ」: Mikanagi: "......This family has always functioned that way."
想子「……ある意味何も変わりはしないのね、ここは……」: Souko: "......In a sense, nothing ever changes here, does it...?"
吐き捨てるように紅緒さんは呟いた。: Benio-san spat out the words in a mutter.
紅緒「……ひとりになってしまったな」: Benio: "......So I've been left all alone."
智士「想子さんも居なくなってしまいましたからね……」: Satoshi: "Since Souko-san is gone as well..."
紅緒「……大丈夫か？」: Benio: "......Are you going to be okay?"
橋の向こうにトラックが来ているらしい。: Apparently, a truck was waiting across the bridge.
麓までは乗せていって貰えるそうなので、それに乗っていこう。: Since we were told we could hitch a ride to the foot of the mountain, let's take it.
暫く歩き、後ろを向く。: After walking for a bit, I turned back.
黒い館の向こうに白い空が見えた。: Beyond the black mansion, I could see a white sky.
この数日間の事を、ぼくは決して忘れないだろう。: I will never forget the events of these past few days.
七月詩音は六曜勇殺害、切原想子は七月藍殺害の容疑で逮捕された。: Shion Nanatsuki was arrested for the murder of Isamu Rokuyou, and Souko Kirihara for the murder of Ai Nanatsuki.
両件共に、高嶺仁弁護士が弁護を担当した。: In both cases, Attorney Jin Takamine handled the defense.
類い希な殺人事件ではあったのだが、叙情酌量の余地が少なかれあったこと等により、量: Although they were rare murder cases, the sentences were lighter than expected, partly due to some room for circumstantial leniency.
それでもふたりは刑務所に服役し、刑期を勤める事となった。: Nevertheless, the two were imprisoned to serve their terms.
両者とも服役中は模範囚だった。: Both were model prisoners during their incarceration.
刑期の軽減もあるだろうと思われていた矢先のことだった。七月詩音に身体検査で妊娠と: It was just when it was thought their sentences might be further reduced. A physical examination revealed that Shion Nanatsuki was pregnant—and had a brain tumor.
妊娠に関し、彼女は誰の子供であるのかを言わなかった。: Regarding the pregnancy, she would not say whose child it was.
脳に出来た腫瘍は――幸いな事に良性種だった。胎児の健康を考えて出産後に手術を行う: The tumor in her brain was—fortunately—benign. Considering the health of the fetus, arrangements were made for surgery to be performed after birth, and she was moved to the prison infirmary.
夏が過ぎ、冬が近付き、詩音は双児を出産した。: Summer passed, winter approached, and Shion gave birth to twins.
そしてすぐさま腫瘍を取り除く手術が行われた。: The surgery to remove the tumor was performed immediately afterward.
しかし――やはり手術が遅かったのだろうか。: However——perhaps the surgery was too late after all.
腫瘍そのものの摘出は成功したが、視神経が損傷しており、彼女は子供の顔を見る事は生: While the removal of the tumor itself was a success, her optic nerves had been damaged, and she was never to see the faces of her children for the rest of her life.
しかしそれでも、彼女は幸せだった。: But even so, she was happy.
仮出獄が認められると、彼女は七月家には帰らず、子供と共に辻村星次の自宅で過ごすよ: When her parole was granted, she did not return to the Nanatsuki house, but began living with her children at Seiji Tsujimura's home.
小さな部屋で、慎ましやかな生活だった。: It was a modest life in a small room.
朝は働きに出る辻村を送る。: In the morning, she would see off Tsujimura as he went to work.
昼は視えぬ眼で子供達の世話をする。: During the day, she cared for the children with eyes that could not see.
夜には帰ってきた辻村と共に夕食を摂る。: At night, she would have dinner with the returning Tsujimura.
初めは慣れぬ生活に戸惑っていた。: At first, she was bewildered by the unfamiliar lifestyle.
それでも周囲の手助けと、子供たちの笑い声に詩音は癒されていた。: Even so, Shion was healed by the help of those around her and the laughter of her children.
休みの日になると、四人揃って郊外の野原へ出掛けた。: On their days off, the four of them would go out to the fields in the suburbs.
一面の菜の花に、子供たちは歓声を上げた。: The children would cheer at the sight of the carpet of canola flowers.
それは昔、スノードロップの花畑を視た詩音と歌音の姿だった。: It was a mirror of Shion and Kanon as they once viewed the field of snowdrops.
あの時は母親がひとりで見守っていた。: Back then, their mother had watched over them alone.
今は、ふたりが子供たちを見守っていた。: Now, the two of them watched over the children together.
たとえ詩音の眼が視えなくても、彼女の脳裏にははしゃぐ我が子の姿がはっきりと映って: Even if Shion's eyes could not see, the image of her frolicking children was clearly reflected in her mind.
詩音は子供たちの笑い声を聞きながら歌を唄った。: Shion sang a song as she listened to her children's laughter.
その歌声は果てしない大空に吸い込まれ――: That singing voice was swallowed into the endless sky——
同じ空を見上げていた切原想子の元へも届くのだった。: And it reached Souko Kirihara, who was looking up at that same sky.
その日も雪が降っていた。: It was snowing on that day as well.
無機質なコンクリの高い塀から、ひとりの女性が出てくる。: A woman emerged from the high, inorganic concrete walls.
刑務官に深く頭を下げ、小さな手提げ袋を持ってこちらを向いたその顔に驚きの表情が浮: She bowed deeply to the prison guard and turned toward me, holding a small handbag; her face was etched with an expression of shock.
ほぼ十年振りに逢った彼女は、少々やつれてはいたものの、美しく年を重ねていた。: Meeting her again after almost ten years, she looked a little haggard, but she had aged beautifully.
「……居るのは当然じゃないですか。待っていたんですから」: "......Isn't it obvious that I'm here? I've been waiting for you."
彼女に向かって傘を差し伸べる。: I held out an umbrella toward her.
肩にそっと手を掛ける。: I gently placed a hand on her shoulder.
そう言うと、彼女は俯いたまま抱きついてきた。: Saying so, she hugged me while still looking down.
答えを返しながら彼女の細い身体を抱き締める。: I replied as I held her slender body tight.
「お帰りなさい、想子さん――車の中にコーラを用意してますよ」: "Welcome home, Souko-san——I've got some cola ready in the car."
「ふふっ……ありがと。気が利くじゃない」: "Heh... thank you. You're quite thoughtful, aren't you?"
あの日とは違う優しい雪が、ゆらゆらと舞い続けていた。: A gentle snow, different from that day, continued to dance softly through the air.
七月詩音は六曜勇殺害の容疑で逮捕された。: Shion Nanatsuki was arrested for the murder of Isamu Rokuyou.
弁護を受け持った高嶺仁弁護士は、心神喪失状態により責任能力は問われないと云う形で: The defense attorney, Jin Takamine, tried to get her off by claiming she was not responsible due to insanity, but she refused.
彼女は素直に取り調べに応じた。裁判もつつがなく進み、多少の軽減はあったものの、懲: She cooperated honestly with the investigation. The trial proceeded smoothly, and although there was some mitigation, she was sentenced to a term of imprisonment.
詩音はすぐ刑務所へ入った。彼女の元へ面会に訪れるのは、七月藍と使用人の辻村星次く: Shion was immediately sent to prison. The only people who came to visit her were Ai Nanatsuki and the servant, Seiji Tsujimura.
服役中の彼女はまさに模範囚だった。刑期の軽減もあるだろうと思われていた矢先のこと: She was truly a model prisoner during her incarceration. It was just when it was thought her sentence might be reduced.
身体検査で妊娠と――脳腫瘍が発見された。: A physical examination revealed that she was pregnant—and had a brain tumor.
それ以上に問題だったのは脳に出来た腫瘍だった。悪性種であり、治療はほぼ不可能であ: The problem more serious than that was the tumor in her brain. It was malignant, and treatment was almost impossible.
詩音は所内の病棟に移され、そこで延命治療を行うことになった。延命とは云え、実際に: Shion was moved to the prison infirmary, where she received life-prolonging treatment. Although it was called life-prolonging, the actual goal was to keep her alive until she gave birth.
そしてその直後、彼女は二十年ほどの短い生涯を終えた。: Immediately afterward, she ended her short life of about twenty years.
残された双児は辻村星次が引き取った。: The remaining twins were taken in by Seiji Tsujimura.
彼も長い間病に罹っていた娘を失っていた。: He, too, had lost a daughter who had been ill for a long time.
彼は七月家を離れ、東京に移り住むそうだ。: He left the Nanatsuki house and reportedly moved to Tokyo.
年が明け、春になった。: $ A new year began, and spring arrived.
切原想子は大学を卒業した後、旅に出ると言い残してふらりと居なくなってしまった。: After graduating from university, Souko Kirihara left a message saying she was going on a journey and vanished.
その後の行方は――誰も知らない。: Her whereabouts since then——no one knows.
ぼくらは東京に戻り、平穏な日常に回帰していた。: We returned to Tokyo and went back to our peaceful daily lives.
夏になり、街を歩いていたぼくの前を見覚えのある人が通り過ぎた。: When summer came, I was walking down the street when a familiar person passed before me.
藍「はい？ あっ……お久し振りです」: Ai: "Yes? Oh... it's been a long time."
智士「こんな所で逢うなんて、珍しいですね」: Satoshi: "It's rare to run into you in a place like this."
藍「ええ、所用がありまして」: Ai: "Yes, I have some business to attend to."
藍「あの……少しお話ししませんか？」: Ai: "Um... would you like to talk for a bit?"
智士「ええ、構いませんよ。やはり本を探しに？」: Satoshi: "Sure, I don't mind. Are you out looking for books again?"
ぼくらが歩いているのは神田の神保町だった。: We were walking in Jinbocho, Kanda.
藍「……詩音に本を差し入れしたいんです」: Ai: "......I want to send some books to Shion."
智士「そうでしたか……どんな本ですか？」: Satoshi: "I see... what kind of books?"
智士「翻訳物でしたら、あちらの書店がいいと思いますよ。大抵のものはそろっています: Satoshi: "For translations, that bookstore over there is good. They have most things in stock."
よかった……随分と落ち着いているようだ。: I'm glad... she seems to have settled down considerably.

――私は泣いていた。男は泣き止んでいた。大の大人が二人でたこ焼きを挟んで泣きあっ: —I was crying. The man had stopped crying. What would a stranger think of the sight of two grown men crying together over takoyaki?

「まぁな、俺はまだ修行中だからな、不味くて当然だ。正直に言ってくれてありがとうよ: "Well, yeah. I'm still in training, so it's only natural they're bad. Thanks for being honest, mister."
私はこの男のことが少しだけわかったような気がした。 ～つづく～: I felt like I understood this man just a little bit. ~To be continued~

『ある男の話・第三話』: "The Tale of a Certain Man - Chapter 3"
その日の公演が終わり、楽屋で一息ついていると、例の屋台の男が大量のたこ焼きを持っ: That day's performance ended, and as I was catching my breath in the dressing room, that man from the food stall arrived with a massive amount of takoyaki.
商売を許可してくれた礼だ、と男はぶっきらぼうに答えた。: "A thanks for lettin' me do business," the man replied bluntly.
しかしその明らかに多すぎるたこ焼きはすでに熱を失い、否が応でも売れ残りであること: However, that clearly excessive amount of takoyaki had already lost its heat, showing whether I liked it or not that they were leftovers.
私は断ろうとしたのだが、遠慮はいらねぇの一点張りで男は聞く耳を持たなかった。: I tried to refuse, but the man insisted there was no need for modesty and wouldn't listen.
正直に言うといらなかったのだ。: To be honest, I didn't want them.
そんなやり取りを暫く続けていたが、根負けした私はついにその禁断のモノを受け取って: We continued that back-and-forth for a while, but finally worn down, I ended up accepting that forbidden thing.
公演終了後で疲れていたのもあって早く終わらせたかったからだ。 ～つづく～: It was because I was tired after the performance and wanted to get it over with quickly. ~To be continued~

今は別に尿意もないしなあ……: I don't really need to go right now...
もよおしたらまた来よう。: I'll come back when the urge strikes.
特に此処に用事はない。: I don't have any particular business here.
ここは前の当主だった恒星氏の部屋だそうだ。: I'm told this is the room of the previous head of the family, Mr. Kousei.
特に何もする事はないんだけど……: I don't really have anything to do, though...
暖炉の火が揺らめいている。: The fire in the fireplace is flickering.
おかげで室内は暖かい。: Thanks to that, the room is warm.
もしこれがなかったとしたら……ぞっとする。: If this wasn't here... I shudder to think.
暖炉のおかげで室内は暖かい。: The room is warm thanks to the fireplace.
隣の部屋と繋がっているのだろうか……: I wonder if it connects to the room next door...
確かにさっき、窓の外に塔のようなものが見えた。: I definitely saw something like a tower outside the window earlier.
もう一度何があるか見てみよう。: Let's see what's out there once more.
ぼくは窓の曇りを拭き取ると顔を近づけた。: I wiped the fog off the window and leaned closer.
吹雪いている風の音とは別の、綺麗な音。: A beautiful sound, distinct from the howling wind of the blizzard.
さっきの歌声……気になるな……: That singing voice from earlier... I'm curious about it...
先ほど一休みしたせいか、シーツにしわが寄っている。: Maybe because I rested a bit earlier, the sheets are wrinkled.
柔らかくてとても寝心地がよかった。: It was soft and very comfortable to sleep in.
結婚式までまだ時間はあるから、もう一眠りしてもいいかもな……: There's still time until the wedding, so maybe I could go back to sleep for a bit...
そうだ、鞄の金具を直すんだった。: Oh right, I was going to fix the metal fitting on my bag.
物置から借りてきたペンチで鞄の留め金を軽く押しつぶす。: I lightly crush the bag's clasp with the pliers I borrowed from the storage room.
ペンチは随分とガタが来ているようで、今にも壊れそうだ。: The pliers seem quite loose, like they're about to break any moment.
鞄のポケットから、小振りのナイフが転がり出てきた。: A small knife tumbled out of my bag's pocket.
そういや、ここに入れてたな……: Oh right, I had put it in here...
特に何かあるわけでもないけれど、一応持っておこう。: It's not like I have a specific use for it, but I'll hold onto it just in case.
医務室に入ると、机に向かっていた御巫先生が顔を上げた。: When I entered the infirmary, Dr. Mikanagi, who was at his desk, looked up.
さて、どうしようか。: Now then, what should I do?
御巫「おや、今度はどうしましたか？」: Mikanagi: "Oh, what is it this time?"
医務室に入ると、御巫先生の姿はなかった。: When I entered the infirmary, Dr. Mikanagi was nowhere to be seen.
薬品の小瓶が並べられている。: Small medicine bottles are lined up.
ぼくの知識ではほとんどどんな薬があるのかわからない。: With my knowledge, I have no idea what most of these medicines are.
いじるわけにもいかないし、そっとしておこう。: I shouldn't mess with them, so I'll just leave them be.
薬品らしきものが入っている。: There's something that looks like medicine inside.
漢方薬にでも使うのか、なにやら木の根のようなものもしまわれているようだ。: I wonder if it's for herbal medicine; something that looks like tree roots is also stored here.
消毒薬や包帯などがおいてあるようだ。: Disinfectants, bandages, and such seem to be placed here.
机の上に何かの書類が置かれている。: Some documents are placed on the desk.
勝手に見るわけにもいかないだろう。: I probably shouldn't look at them without permission.
窓の外は雪が舞っている。: Snow is dancing outside the window.
風も随分と強くなっているようだ。: The wind seems to have grown quite strong.
ぼくが入ってきたのとは別の扉がある。: There's another door, separate from the one I entered through.
恐らく隣の書庫と繋がっているのだろう。: It probably connects to the adjacent archive.
他の部屋にあるのとは全く別の簡素な寝台が置かれている。: A simple bed, completely different from the ones in the other rooms, is placed here.
まあ、医務室だしそうだよな……: Well, it's an infirmary, so that makes sense...
さすがに勝手に見るわけにもいかないよな……: I really shouldn't look at them without permission...
一番下の引き出しに、綺麗なカードが入っていた。: In the bottom drawer, I found a pretty card.
カードには『ｒ』と書かれていた。: The card had the letter 'r' written on it.
ここは確か書庫だった筈だ。: I'm pretty sure this was the archive.
鍵が掛けられているとばかり思っていたので、これはちょっと拍子抜けだった。: Since I fully expected it to be locked, this was a bit of an anticlimax.
まあ、普段使う部屋だとするならば、自宅の中で鍵を掛ける事もないか。: Well, if it's a room used regularly, I guess there's no need to lock it inside one's own home.
部屋の中は大きな本棚でほぼ占められていた。: The room was almost entirely occupied by large bookshelves.
ここは調べがいがありそうだけれど……: This place looks like it's worth investigating, but...
塔へ行く前に、まずは事務室で辻村さんに話を訊いてみよう。: Before going to the tower, I should first try asking Tsujimura-san in the office.
塔に行くには、まず辻村さんに許可を貰うべきだろう。: I should probably get permission from Tsujimura-san before going to the tower.
事務室の奥にある扉から外に出て、雪が降り続いている庭を歩いて塔へ近付く。: I go out through the door in the back of the office and walk through the garden where the snow continues to fall to approach the tower.
強い吹雪の所為か、歌声は聞こえない。: Likely due to the heavy blizzard, I can't hear the singing.
塔の根本にある扉は開いていた。: The door at the base of the tower was open.
中に入ると、頭上から歌声が聞こえてきた。: When I stepped inside, I heard a singing voice from above.
内部はがらんとしていて、壁に沿うように階段が上へ向かっている。: The interior was empty, with stairs winding upwards along the wall.
ぼくは階段を上ってみる事にした。: I decided to try going up the stairs.
階段を上り切った所に扉があった。: At the top of the stairs was a door.
ぼくは暫くの間、ここで彼女の歌を聴いていた。: I stood there for a while, listening to her song.
そこでぼくは初めて、扉を軽くノックした。: It was then that I lightly knocked on the door for the first time.
智士「あの、すみません。こちらのお屋敷に助けていただいた(NAME01)(NAM: Satoshi: "Um, excuse me. My name is Kobayashi Satoshi, and I was rescued by this household. I wanted to offer a word of greeting."
？？？「そうですか……私が言える立場ではありませんが、どうぞごゆっくりしていって: ???: "I see... I'm not in a position to say, but please, make yourself at home."
やはり警戒されているのだろう、中から扉を開けてくれるような気配はない。: She must be on guard; there's no sign of her opening the door from the inside.
智士「ええ。……それで、先程から歌を唄っていたようですが」: Satoshi: "Yes. ...So, it sounded like you were singing just now."
智士「綺麗な歌声ですね」: Satoshi: "You have a beautiful singing voice."
智士「あ、あとご結婚おめでとうございます」: Satoshi: "Oh, and congratulations on your wedding."
智士「じゃあ、ぼくはこれで。……失礼します」: Satoshi: "Well then, I'll be going now. ...Excuse me."
なんだか元気がないように思えたのが気になったが、ぼくはこの場を離れる事にした。: I was concerned that she seemed somewhat listless, but I decided to leave the area.
外に出てぼくは塔を見上げた。: Once outside, I looked up at the tower.
どうしてこんな離れた所に彼女は居るのだろうか。: Why is she in a place so far removed?
もう一度彼女の声が聞きたい。: I want to hear her voice once more.
塔に入り、階段を上っていく。: I enter the tower and head up the stairs.
智士「少しお話でもと思ったんですけれど……」: Satoshi: "I thought we might talk for a bit..."
？？？「……申し訳ありませんが、式の準備がありますので……」: ???: "...I'm sorry, but I have preparations for the ceremony..."
智士「あ、そうですよね、お邪魔してすみませんでした」: Satoshi: "Ah, right. Sorry for bothering you."
ぼくはそそくさと退散する事にした。: I decided to make a hasty retreat.

辻村「この雪では私も雪かきをしなくてはなりませんね。詩音お嬢様が転んでは大変です: Tsujimura: "In this snow, I'll have to do some shoveling myself. It would be a disaster if Lady Shion were to trip."
六曜「詩音が居る塔だろう？ あそこには門番がいるからね、迂闊に近づけないんだよ」: Rokuyou: "The tower where Shion is, right? There's a gatekeeper there, so you can't just approach it carelessly."
高嶺「外の塔か……見に行きたいのならば辻村さんに頼むといい」: Takamine: "The tower outside... if you want to see it, you should ask Mr. Tsujimura."
高嶺「……詩音君はずっと以前からあの塔に住んでいるそうだ」: Takamine: "...I hear Shion-kun has lived in that tower since a long time ago."
御巫「塔ね……事務室の裏の扉から行けるよ」: Mikanagi: "The tower... you can get there through the back door in the office."
御巫「詩音さんは身体が弱いから離れで暮らしているそうだけれど……僕の父がそう指示: Mikanagi: "I've heard Shion-san lives in the annex because she's physically weak... but it might have been my father who gave those instructions..."
あかね「外の塔ですか？ 詩音お嬢様のお部屋ですけれど……」: Akane: "The tower outside? That is Lady Shion's room..."
あかね「詩音お嬢様にはわたしがお食事を持っていくんですが、大抵は事務室で召し上が: Akane: "I am the one who brings Lady Shion her meals, but she usually eats them in the office."
なるみ「外の塔には末娘の詩音お嬢様が住んでいるのよ」: Narumi: "The youngest daughter, Lady Shion, lives in that tower outside."
なるみ「詩音お嬢様だけが離れで暮らしているのよね……何だか奇妙よね」: Narumi: "Only Lady Shion lives in the annex... it's a bit strange, isn't it?"
想子「聖堂まで建物の中にあるんだものね……さすがお金持ちは違うわよね」: Souko: "To think there's even a chapel inside the building... the wealthy really are something else."
摩夜「この建物が出来た当初から聖堂はあったそうですわ」: Maya: "I believe the chapel has been here since this building was first constructed."
藍「あの聖堂は七月家の結婚式には必ず使われていたそうですよ」: Ai: "I've heard that chapel has always been used for the Nanatsuki family's weddings."
紅緒「聖堂の内部は後ほど御覧になれますわよ」: Benio: "You will be able to see the inside of the chapel later."
辻村「今は式の準備をしておりますので中は御覧になれません」: Tsujimura: "Preparations for the ceremony are underway now, so you cannot see the inside."
六曜「そう焦らなくても、後で中には入れるよ」: Rokuyou: "No need to rush; you'll be able to go inside later."
高嶺「式の準備をしているから入れないぞ」: Takamine: "They're preparing for the ceremony, so you can't go in."
御巫「聖堂は今結婚式の準備をしているんじゃないですか？」: Mikanagi: "Aren't they preparing for the wedding in the chapel right now?"
あかね「聖堂はお掃除が大変なんですよ。普段は使っていませんからそう汚れたりはしな: Akane: "The chapel is quite a chore to clean. We don't use it normally, so it doesn't get very dirty, though."
なるみ「結婚式にしか使わない聖堂なんて、なんだか贅沢よね」: Narumi: "A chapel that's only used for weddings... it's a bit of a luxury, isn't it?"
想子「歌……？ 聞いてないからわからないわね」: Souko: "A song...? I haven't heard anything, so I wouldn't know."
摩夜「歌ですか……恐らく詩音が歌っているのでしょう」: Maya: "A song? It is likely Shion who is singing."
藍「それは詩音が歌っているのでしょうね。彼女の声はとても綺麗ですから」: Ai: "That would be Shion singing, I imagine. Her voice is quite beautiful."
紅緒「歌……恐らく詩音ではありませんか？」: Benio: "A song... that would likely be Shion, wouldn't it?"
辻村「それは恐らく詩音お嬢様でしょうね。よくおひとりで歌っておられますから」: Tsujimura: "That would likely be Lady Shion. She often sings to herself."
辻村「この事務室の裏手の扉から詩音お嬢様が住まわれるお部屋へ行けます」: Tsujimura: "You can reach the room where Lady Shion resides through this door at the back of the office."
智士「あの、よろしければご挨拶に窺いたいのですが……」: Satoshi: "Um, if it's alright, I'd like to go and offer my greetings..."
辻村「ええ、それは構いませんが……」: Tsujimura: "Yes, I don't mind that, but..."
六曜「歌？ ……そういえば聞こえたような気もするね」: Rokuyou: "A song? ...Come to think of it, I feel like I might have heard something."
高嶺「歌か……誰かが歌っているのだろうな」: Takamine: "A song... someone must be singing."
御巫「歌ねえ……恐らく詩音さんだと思うよ」: Mikanagi: "A song... I believe that would be Shion-san."
あかね「歌ですか……よく詩音お嬢様が歌われているようですが……」: Akane: "A song? Lady Shion seems to sing quite often, but..."
なるみ「よく歌ってるのは詩音お嬢様かしら。紅緒お嬢様もピアノを弾いたりするようだ: Narumi: "The one who sings often would be Lady Shion, I think. Though Lady Benio plays the piano as well."
想子「演奏室に置いてあったわね。さすがに年代物のようだったけれど……」: Souko: "It was sitting in the performance room. It certainly looked like an antique..."
摩夜「あれは主人が戦前に買い求めた物です。わざわざドイツから輸入したそうですわ」: Maya: "That is something my husband purchased before the war. It was imported all the way from Germany."
藍「私は弾きませんが……紅緒はよく弾いていますよ」: Ai: "I do not play it myself... but Benio plays quite often."
紅緒「ピアノは父上ではなく、辻村に習いましたのよ。今でもたまに弾いておりますわ」: Benio: "I didn't learn the piano from my father, but from Tsujimura. I still play it from time to time."
辻村「……恥ずかしながら私も少しだけ嗜んでいた事がありまして、旦那様がお忙しかっ: Tsujimura: "...I'm embarrassed to say I used to dabble in it a bit myself. Since the Master was busy, I was the one who taught Lady Benio."
六曜「ピアノ？ 恐らく先代のものだろうね。どのくらいの価値があるかはしらないけれ: Rokuyou: "A piano? It likely belonged to the previous head. I don't know how much it's worth, though..."
高嶺「別に弾いても構わないと思うが、壊さないように」: Takamine: "I don't think anyone would mind if you played it, but don't break it."
御巫「ピアノか……随分と古い物らしいね。恒星さんのお気に入りの楽器だったようだよ: Mikanagi: "A piano? It's apparently quite old. It seems to have been one of Mr. Kousei's favorite instruments."
あかね「演奏室にあるピアノですね。いつも綺麗に磨いていますよ」: Akane: "The piano in the performance room, right. I always make sure to polish it until it shines."
なるみ「演奏室のピアノ？ 紅緒お嬢様くらいしか弾いているのを見たことないわね」: Narumi: "The piano in the performance room? I've never seen anyone but Lady Benio playing it."
想子「私について聞きたいの？ 知ってるじゃない」: Souko: "You want to hear about me? You already know, don't you?"
智士「いや、そこを何とか」: Satoshi: "No, come on, give me something."
想子「きみの大学の先輩で無類のコーラ好き。これでいい？」: Souko: "Your university senior and an unparalleled Cola lover. Will that do?"
想子「じゃあ、民俗学者でここへは調査のために来たとでも言っておく？」: Souko: "Well then, shall I say I'm a folklorist who came here for research?"
智士「想子さんだってまだ学生じゃないですか」: Satoshi: "Souko-san, you're still a student too."
摩夜「切原さん？ ごめんなさいね、よく知りませんわ」: Maya: "Kirihara-san? I'm sorry, I don't know her well."
藍「切原さんですか？ 会ったばかりですし、よくわかりませんが……」: Ai: "Kirihara-san? We've only just met, so I'm not sure..."
紅緒「切原さんでございますか？ ……申し訳ありませんが、よく存じておりませんので: Benio: "Kirihara-san? ...I am sorry, I am not well acquainted with her..."
辻村「切原様ですか……よく存じておりませんが……」: Tsujimura: "Ms. Kirihara? ...I don't know her well, but..."
六曜「切原さん……ああ、彼女か。僕よりもきみの方が知っているだろう？」: Rokuyou: "Kirihara-san... ah, her. You probably know more about her than I do, don't you?"
高嶺「切原……ああ、彼女のことか。俺が何か知っている筈もないだろう？」: Takamine: "Kirihara... ah, her. There's no reason I would know anything, is there?"
あかね「切原様ですか？ お部屋にいらっしゃるのではないですか？」: Akane: "Ms. Kirihara? I believe she would be in her room."
なるみ「切原様？ ……さあ、しりませんよ」: Narumi: "Ms. Kirihara? ...No, I don't know her."
想子「摩夜さんねえ……いかにも奥様って感じよね。優しそうではあるけれど」: Souko: "Maya-san, huh... she definitely has that air of a mistress about her. She seems kind, though."
摩夜「私の事ですか……こんなおばさんの事なんて知っても面白くないでしょう？」: Maya: "About me? Surely it wouldn't be interesting to know about an old woman like me?"
智士「いえ……そんな事はないんですが……」: Satoshi: "No... that's not true at all..."
紅緒「母上で御座いますか？ 温厚で優しい方ですわ」: Benio: "My mother? She is a gentle and kind person."
辻村「奥様ですか……大変お優しい方ですよ」: Tsujimura: "The Mistress? She's a very kind person."
六曜「摩夜さんかい？ 今回の結婚式にも協力して貰えたので感謝しているよ」: Rokuyou: "Maya-san? I'm grateful for her cooperation with this wedding."
高嶺「摩夜さんか……恒星氏が亡くなってから女手ひとつで頑張っておられるようだ」: Takamine: "Maya-san... she seems to have done her best on her own since Mr. Kousei passed away."
御巫「摩夜さんね……慈愛に満ちた方だと思うよ」: Mikanagi: "Maya-san... I believe she is a person full of affection."
あかね「奥様ですか？ 恐らくお部屋にいらっしゃると思いますが……」: Akane: "The Mistress? I believe she is in her room, but..."
なるみ「奥様？ 優しくていい人よ」: Narumi: "The Mistress? She's a kind and good person."
想子「藍さんについて？ いかにもお嬢様、って感じよね」: Souko: "About Ai-san? She definitely feels like a proper young lady."
摩夜「藍ですか？ 大人しい子で、いつも読書をしていますわ」: Maya: "Ai? She's a quiet girl who's always lost in her books."
藍「私の事ですか？ ええと……特にお話しするような事はありませんが……」: Ai: "About me? Um... I don't really have anything to talk about, but..."
紅緒「姉上でございますか？ 物静かで良い姉ですわ」: Benio: "My sister? She is a quiet and good elder sister."
辻村「藍お嬢様ですか……いつも本を読んでいらっしゃいますね」: Tsujimura: "Lady Ai? She's always reading her books."
六曜「藍の事かい？ 僕とは幼なじみで仲は良い方だと思うよ」: Rokuyou: "About Ai? We're childhood friends, so I'd say we're on good terms."
高嶺「藍君について？ 昔から大人しい少女だったようだが……」: Takamine: "About Ai-kun? She's been a quiet girl since she was small, but..."
御巫「藍さんか……偶に感情の起伏が激しいときがあるね」: Mikanagi: "Ai-san, huh... she has times where her emotions can be quite intense."
あかね「藍お嬢様ですか？ 今でしたらご自分のお部屋にいらっしゃるのではないでしょ: Akane: "Lady Ai? I believe she would be in her room right now."
なるみ「藍お嬢様？ よく書庫で本を読んでいるわよ」: Narumi: "Lady Ai? She's often in the archive reading books."
想子「紅緒さん？ ……さあ、まだよく解らないわね。なんだか猫を被ってるようだけど: Souko: "Benio-san? ...Well, I still don't quite have a read on her. It feels like she's putting on an act, though..."
摩夜「紅緒ですか？ 少々変わっておりますが、良い子ですわよ」: Maya: "Benio? She's a bit peculiar, but she's a good girl."
藍「紅緒についてですか？ 少々変わった処はありますけど、悪い子ではありませんよ」: Ai: "About Benio? She has her quirks, but she isn't a bad child."
紅緒「私で御座いますか？ ……特にお話しする事はありませんが……」: Benio: "About me? ...I don't really have much to tell you..."
辻村「紅緒お嬢様ですか……特に私から申し上げる事は御座いませんが……」: Tsujimura: "Lady Benio? ...I have nothing in particular to say on that matter..."
六曜「紅緒かい？ とても良い子だよ。少々変わっているけれどね」: Rokuyou: "Benio? She's a very sweet girl. A bit eccentric, perhaps."
高嶺「紅緒君か……まあ風変わりではあるが……」: Takamine: "Benio-kun... well, she is certainly unconventional..."
あかね「紅緒お嬢様ですか？ お部屋にいらっしゃるのではありませんか？」: Akane: "Lady Benio? Wouldn't she be in her room?"
なるみ「紅緒お嬢様？ ……まあ、あたしの口から何か言う事はないわね」: Narumi: "Lady Benio? ...Well, it's not my place to say anything."
想子「詩音さん？ ……会っていないから何とも言えないわ。まあ焦らなくても夜中の結: Souko: "Shion-san? ...I haven't met her, so I can't really say. Though I'm sure we'll meet at the midnight wedding, so there's no rush."
摩夜「詩音ですか？ 昔から身体が弱くて、雑菌などで病気に感染するおそれがありまし: Maya: "Shion? She's been frail since she was small, and we were afraid of her catching some sort of infection, so we have her living in the annex."
藍「詩音は……生まれてからずっと館の外へ出たことはありません。その所為か、あまり: Ai: "Shion... she hasn't set foot outside the manor since she was born. Perhaps because of that, she doesn't try to speak with people much."
紅緒「詩音は大変頭の良い子ですわ。ただ……経験が圧倒的に足りないのですけれど」: Benio: "Shion is an incredibly bright child. However... she is desperately lacking in experience."
辻村「詩音お嬢様ですか……とても綺麗な歌を唄われますよ。私もよく聞き惚れて……い: Tsujimura: "Lady Shion? ...She sings such beautiful songs. I often find myself listening in a daze... ah, how embarrassing..."
六曜「詩音のことかい？ 心優しいところに惹かれてね……はは、恥ずかしいな改めてこ: Rokuyou: "About Shion? I was drawn to her gentle heart... haha, it's a bit embarrassing to talk about this so openly..."
高嶺「詩音君か……俺が此処に来るようになる前からずっとあの塔で生活をしているよう: Takamine: "Shion-kun... she seems to have lived in that tower since long before I started coming here."
御巫「詩音さんは身体が弱くて頭痛持ちだから、毎日それを抑える薬を飲むんだ。何とか: Mikanagi: "Shion-san is frail and suffers from headaches, so she takes medicine to suppress them every day. I only wish she would get better..."
あかね「詩音お嬢様ですか？ いつも外の塔に居られますよ。事務室の奥の扉から外に出: Akane: "Lady Shion? She is always in the tower outside. You can go out through the door at the back of the office."
なるみ「詩音お嬢様？ あまりご飯を食べられないようなのよ。少しは栄養付けないとい: Narumi: "Lady Shion? She doesn't seem to be able to eat much. She really needs to get some nutrition into her system..."
想子「辻村さん？ 恒星氏の弟さんなのにここでは小間使いみたいな事していて……どこ: Souko: "Tsujimura-san? Even though he's Mr. Kousei's younger brother, he acts like a servant here... it's a bit strange."
摩夜「辻村ですか？ 夫の弟でしたから、こちらで雇ったのですわ。よく働いてください: Maya: "Tsujimura? He was my husband's younger brother, so we hired him. He works hard, and I am very grateful to him."
藍「辻村さんですか？ 私たちの叔父にあたる方ですが、とても優しい方ですよ」: Ai: "Tsujimura-san? He is our uncle, but he's a very kind man."
辻村「私の事ですか……？ いや、そう胸を張って言えるような事など何もありませんよ: Tsujimura: "About me...? No, I have nothing to say that I could be proud of."
六曜「辻村のこと？ ……さあ、亡くなった当主の弟と云う事くらいしかしらないな……: Rokuyou: "About Tsujimura? ...Well, I only know that he's the younger brother of the deceased Master..."
高嶺「辻村さんの事か？ 先代の弟で、今はここの管理人のような事をしているそうだが: Takamine: "About Tsujimura-san? He's the previous head's younger brother and currently acts as the caretaker here, I hear."
御巫「辻村さんは七月家の事務作業を一手に引き受けている人だよ。執事と云ってもいい: Mikanagi: "Tsujimura-san is the one who handles all the paperwork for the Nanatsuki family. You could even call him a butler."
あかね「辻村さんですか？ 普段は事務室でお仕事をされていますよ」: Akane: "Tsujimura-san? He usually works in the office."
なるみ「辻村さん？ 優しい人よ。少々気が弱い所もあるけれど……」: Narumi: "Tsujimura-san? He's a kind man. A bit timid at times, though..."
想子「高嶺さん？ 何か厳つい人よね。弁護士とは思えないわ」: Souko: "Takamine-san? He's quite a stern man, isn't he? He doesn't seem like a lawyer at all."
摩夜「高嶺弁護士は父親も七月の顧問弁護士でした。とても仕事には熱心な方ですわ」: Maya: "Attorney Takamine's father was also the Nanatsuki family's legal counsel. He is very dedicated to his work."
藍「高嶺さんはお父上に連れられて、小さな頃からここへ来ていたそうですよ」: Ai: "Mr. Takamine has apparently been coming here since he was small, brought along by his father."
紅緒「高嶺様で御座いますか？ 寡黙な方ですわね……それに顔の火傷の痕がとても痛々: Benio: "Mr. Takamine? He is a man of few words... and the burn scars on his face look quite painful."
辻村「高嶺様ですか。本日は結婚式の際に立ち会っていただくつもりです。弁護士ですか: Tsujimura: "Mr. Takamine. He is here to witness the wedding today. As a lawyer, he's necessary for the contract signing."
六曜「高嶺さんかい？ 代々七月家の顧問弁護士をしているそうだけど、あまり話したこ: Rokuyou: "Takamine-san? I hear his family has been legal counsel for the Nanatsuki for generations, but I haven't talked to him much."
御巫「高嶺さんのことかい？ 僕と同じで、父親がこの家に関わっていたようだね」: Mikanagi: "About Mr. Takamine? Just like me, his father seems to have been involved with this family."
あかね「高嶺様ですか……恐らくお部屋にいらっしゃると思いますが……」: Akane: "Mr. Takamine? I believe he would be in his room, but..."
なるみ「高嶺さん？ ……おっかない顔よね。あれで弁護士の先生だっていうんだから…: Narumi: "Takamine-san? ...He has a scary face, doesn't he? To think a guy like that is a lawyer... you just never know with this world."
想子「御巫先生のこと？ うーん、指の手当はしてもらったけど、なんというか軽そうよ: Souko: "About Dr. Mikanagi? Hmm, he did treat my finger, but he feels a bit... flighty, I guess."
摩夜「御巫先生のお父様には随分とお世話になりました。今の私があるのも御巫先生達の: Maya: "We were very much indebted to Dr. Mikanagi's father. I am only where I am today thanks to the Mikanagi family."
藍「御巫先生にはよくお世話になっています。こんな辺鄙な所までお医者様は来られませ: Ai: "Dr. Mikanagi often looks after us. Doctors won't come out to such a remote place, so it's a huge help."
紅緒「はかせの事で御座いますか？ 昔からこちらにいらっしゃいますから、もう家族同: Benio: "Are you asking about Hiroshi? He's been here since long ago, so we're practically like family."
辻村「御巫先生はここになくてはならない方ですから。奥様や詩音お嬢様はあまり身体が: Tsujimura: "Dr. Mikanagi is indispensable here. The Mistress and Lady Shion aren't in the best of health, after all..."
六曜「博士とは昔からの腐れ縁だから色々と知っているよ。医大に居た頃も何人もの女性: Rokuyou: "Hiroshi and I have been stuck with each other for years, so I know a lot. Even back in med school, he seemed to be breaking plenty of hearts."
高嶺「御巫先生は優れた医者のようだ。本来は内科だが、簡単な外科手術ならば出来るだ: Takamine: "Dr. Mikanagi appears to be an excellent physician. He specializes in internal medicine, but I'm sure he could handle simple surgeries if needed."
御巫「僕かい？ 本当の七月家の主治医は父なんだよ。でも高齢でね、近頃は僕がずっと: Mikanagi: "Me? My father is the true family doctor for the Nanatsuki. But he's getting on in years, so I've been staying here lately."
あかね「えっ、御巫先生ですかっ！？ えと、そのっ……医務室にいらっしゃるのではな: Akane: "Eh, Dr. Mikanagi!? Um, well... wouldn't he be in the infirmary...?"
なるみ「御巫先生？ どこか軽い雰囲気よね。まああたしに声を掛けるなんて事ほとんど: Narumi: "Dr. Mikanagi? He has a bit of a light air about him. Though he hardly ever says a word to me."
想子「あかねさん？ 真面目そうでいい子よね」: Souko: "Akane-san? she seems like a serious, good girl."
摩夜「あかねはよく働いてくれますわ。細かいところにもよく気付きますし」: Maya: "Akane works very hard. She's very attentive to the smallest details."
紅緒「あかねさんでございますか？ とてもよい方ですわよ。少々抜けている所もありま: Benio: "Akane-san? She is a very good person. A bit scatterbrained at times, but..."
辻村「燈山さんですか？ 仕事はきちんとこなされますし、問題は特にありませんが……: Tsujimura: "Ms. Hiyama? She handles her work properly, and I have no complaints..."
六曜「あかねかい？ 少々抜けている所はあるようだけど、頑張っているようじゃないか: Rokuyou: "Akane? She seems a bit flighty, but she's giving it her all, isn't she?"

ぼくは一足先に大広間へ行く事にした。: I decided to head to the grand hall ahead of them.
犯人だってひとりでいる人を見境無く襲ったりはしない筈だ。: Even the culprit wouldn't just attack anyone they see alone indiscriminately.
……そうじゃないと、今までにもう何人も被害に遭っている筈だ。: ...If that weren't the case, many more people would have been victims by now.
ぼくは胸に一抹の不安を抱えながらも大広間に向かう事にした。: Despite a lingering unease in my chest, I decided to head to the grand hall.
